165.section_title: Public servant obtaining any valuable thing, without consideration, from person concerned in any proceeding or business transacted by such public servant

raw.marginal_note: Public servant obtaining any valuable thing, without consideration, from person concerned in any proceeding or business transacted by such public servant
raw.text: Whoever, being a public servant, accepts or obtains, or agrees to accept or attempts to obtain, for himself or for any other person, any valuable thing, without consideration, or for a consideration which he knows to be inadequate, from any person whom he knows to have been, or to be, or to be likely to be concerned in any proceedings or business transacted, or about to be transacted, by such public servant, or having any connection with the official functions of himself or of any public servant to whom he is subordinate, or from any person whom he knows to be interested in or related to the person so concerned, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 2 years, or with fine, or with both.

sub_items.text: Illustrations

sub_items.label: a
sub_items.text: ( a ) A , a judge, hires a house of Z , who has a case pending before him. It is agreed that A shall pay $50 a month, the house being such that, if the bargain were made in good faith, A would be required to pay $200 a month. A has obtained a valuable thing from Z without adequate consideration.

sub_items.label: b
sub_items.text: ( b ) A , a judge, buys of Z , who has a cause pending in A ’s court, Government promissory notes at a discount, when they are selling in the market at a premium. A has obtained a valuable thing from Z without adequate consideration.

sub_items.label: c
sub_items.text: ( c ) Z ’s brother is apprehended and taken before A , a Magistrate, on a charge of perjury. A sells to Z shares in a bank at a premium, when they are selling in the market at a discount. Z pays A for the shares accordingly. The money so obtained by A is a valuable thing obtained by him without adequate consideration.

166.section_title: Public servant disobeying a direction of the law, with intent to cause injury to any person

raw.marginal_note: Public servant disobeying a direction of the law, with intent to cause injury to any person
raw.text: Whoever, being a public servant, knowingly disobeys any direction of the law as to the way in which he is to conduct himself as such public servant, intending to cause, or knowing it to be likely that he will, by such disobedience, cause injury to any person, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both.

sub_items.text: Illustration

A , being an officer directed by law to take property under or pursuant to an enforcement order, or in execution in order to satisfy a decree, pronounced in Z ’s favour by a court of justice, knowingly disobeys that direction of law, with the knowledge that he is likely thereby to cause injury to Z . A has committed the offence defined in this section.

167.section_title: Public servant framing an incorrect document or electronic record with intent to cause injury

raw.marginal_note: Public servant framing an incorrect document or electronic record with intent to cause injury
raw.text: Whoever, being a public servant, and being, as such public servant, charged with the preparation or translation of any document or electronic record, frames or translates that document or electronic record in a manner which he knows or believes to be incorrect, intending thereby to cause, or knowing it to be likely that he may thereby cause, injury to any person, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 3 years, or with fine, or with both.

168.section_title: Public servant unlawfully engaging in trade

raw.marginal_note: Public servant unlawfully engaging in trade
raw.text: Whoever, being a public servant, and being legally bound as such public servant not to engage in trade, engages in trade, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both.

169.section_title: Public servant unlawfully buying or bidding for property

raw.marginal_note: Public servant unlawfully buying or bidding for property
raw.text: Whoever, being a public servant, and being legally bound as such public servant not to purchase or bid for certain property, purchases or bids for that property, either in his own name or in the name of another, or jointly or in shares with others, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 2 years, or with fine, or with both; and the property, if purchased, shall be confiscated.

171.section_title: Wearing garb or carrying token used by public servant, with fraudulent intent

raw.marginal_note: Wearing garb or carrying token used by public servant, with fraudulent intent
raw.text: Whoever, not belonging to a certain class of public servants, wears any garb or carries any token resembling any garb or token used by that class of public servants, with the intention that it may be believed, or with the knowledge that it is likely to be believed, that he belongs to that class of public servants, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 6 months, or with fine which may extend to $1,000, or with both.

172.section_title: Absconding to avoid arrest on warrant or service of summons, etc., proceeding from a public servant

raw.marginal_note: Absconding to avoid arrest on warrant or service of summons, etc., proceeding from a public servant
raw.text: Whoever absconds in order to avoid being arrested on a warrant, or to avoid being served with a summons, a notice, or an order proceeding from any public servant, legally competent, as such public servant, to issue such warrant, summons, notice or order, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to $1,500, or with both; or, if the summons, notice or order is to attend in person or by agent, or to produce a document or an electronic record before a court of justice, with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 6 months, or with fine which may extend to $5,000, or with both.

          Circumstance: The summons, notice or order is to attend in person or by agent, or to produce a document or an electronic record before a court of justice

Penalty:
  Shall be punished with imprisonment for a term of not less than no time and not more than 6 months, or with a fine of not less than S$0.00 and not more than S$5,000.00, or with any combination thereof.

metadata.summary: This section makes it an offence to abscond in order to avoid arrest on a warrant or service of process issued by a legally competent public servant, with a higher penalty for specified court process.

173.section_title: Preventing service of summons, etc., or preventing publication thereof

raw.marginal_note: Preventing service of summons, etc., or preventing publication thereof
raw.text: —(1) A person who in any manner — ( a ) intentionally prevents the serving on himself, or on any other person, of any summons, notice or order, proceeding from any public servant legally competent, as such public servant, to issue such summons, notice or order; ( b ) intentionally prevents the lawful affixing to any place of any such summons, notice or order, or intentionally removes any such summons, notice or order from any place to which it is lawfully affixed; or ( c ) intentionally prevents the lawful making of any proclamation, under the authority of any public servant legally competent, as such public servant, to direct such proclamation to be made, shall — ( d ) in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to $1,500, or with both; or ( e ) in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000. (2) If the summons, notice, order, or proclamation is to attend in person or by agent before a court, or to produce a document or an electronic record before a court, any person who is guilty of an offence under subsection (1) shall —

sub_items.text: —(1) A person who in any manner — ( a ) intentionally prevents the serving on himself, or on any other person, of any summons, notice or order, proceeding from any public servant legally competent, as such public servant, to issue such summons, notice or order; ( b ) intentionally prevents the lawful affixing to any place of any such summons, notice or order, or intentionally removes any such summons, notice or order from any place to which it is lawfully affixed; or ( c ) intentionally prevents the lawful making of any proclamation, under the authority of any public servant legally competent, as such public servant, to direct such proclamation to be made, shall — ( d ) in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to $1,500, or with both; or ( e ) in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000.

sub_items.label: (a)

sub_items.label: (b)

sub_items.label: (c)
sub_items.text: intentionally prevents the lawful making of any proclamation, under the authority of any public servant legally competent, as such public servant, to direct such proclamation to be made,

sub_items.label: (d)
sub_items.text: in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to $1,500, or with both; or

sub_items.label: (e)
sub_items.text: in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000.

(2) If the summons, notice, order, or proclamation is to attend in person or by agent before a court, or to produce a document or an electronic record before a court, any person who is guilty of an offence under subsection (1) shall — ( a ) in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 6 months, or with fine which may extend to $5,000, or with both; or ( b ) in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000.

sub_items.label: (2)(a)
sub_items.text: in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 6 months, or with fine which may extend to $5,000, or with both; or

sub_items.label: (2)(b)
sub_items.text: in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000.

174.section_title: Failure to attend in obedience to order from public servant

raw.marginal_note: Failure to attend in obedience to order from public servant
raw.text: —(1) A person who, being legally bound to attend in person or by an agent at a certain place and time in obedience to a summons, a notice, an order or a proclamation, proceeding from any public servant legally competent, as such public servant, to issue the same, intentionally omits to attend at the place or time, or departs from the place where the person is bound to attend before the time at which it is lawful for the person to depart, shall — ( a ) in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to $1,500, or with both; or ( b ) in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000. (2) If the summons, notice, order or proclamation is to attend in person or by agent before a court, any person who is guilty of an offence under subsection (1) shall —

sub_items.text: —(1) A person who, being legally bound to attend in person or by an agent at a certain place and time in obedience to a summons, a notice, an order or a proclamation, proceeding from any public servant legally competent, as such public servant, to issue the same, intentionally omits to attend at the place or time, or departs from the place where the person is bound to attend before the time at which it is lawful for the person to depart, shall — ( a ) in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to $1,500, or with both; or ( b ) in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000.

sub_items.label: (a)
sub_items.text: in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to $1,500, or with both; or

sub_items.label: (b)
sub_items.text: in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000.

(2) If the summons, notice, order or proclamation is to attend in person or by agent before a court, any person who is guilty of an offence under subsection (1) shall — ( a ) in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 6 months, or with fine which may extend to $5,000, or with both; or ( b ) in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000.

sub_items.label: (2)(a)
sub_items.text: in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 6 months, or with fine which may extend to $5,000, or with both; or

sub_items.label: (2)(b)
sub_items.text: in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000.
Illustrations

sub_items.label: a
sub_items.text: ( a ) A , being legally bound to appear before the General Division of the High Court, in obedience to an order to attend court issuing from the General Division of the High Court, intentionally omits to appear. A has committed the offence defined in this section.

sub_items.label: b
sub_items.text: ( b ) A , being legally bound to appear before a Magistrate as a witness, in obedience to a summons issued by that Magistrate, intentionally omits to appear. A has committed the offence defined in this section.

175.section_title: Omission to produce document or electronic record to public servant by person legally bound to produce such document or electronic record

raw.marginal_note: Omission to produce document or electronic record to public servant by person legally bound to produce such document or electronic record
raw.text: A person who, being legally bound to produce or deliver up any document or electronic record to any public servant, as such, intentionally omits so to produce or deliver up the same, shall — ( a ) in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to $1,500, or with both; or ( b ) in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000.

176.section_title: Omission to give notice or information to public servant by person legally bound to give such notice or information

raw.marginal_note: Omission to give notice or information to public servant by person legally bound to give such notice or information
raw.text: —(1) A person who, being legally bound to give any notice or to furnish information on any subject to any public servant, as such, intentionally omits to give such notice or to furnish such information in the manner and at the time required by law, shall — ( a ) in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to $1,500, or with both; or ( b ) in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000. (2) If the notice or information required to be given respects the commission of an offence, or is required for the purpose of preventing the commission of an offence or in order to the apprehension of an offender, any person who is guilty of an offence under subsection (1) shall —

sub_items.text: —(1) A person who, being legally bound to give any notice or to furnish information on any subject to any public servant, as such, intentionally omits to give such notice or to furnish such information in the manner and at the time required by law, shall — ( a ) in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to $1,500, or with both; or ( b ) in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000.

(2) If the notice or information required to be given respects the commission of an offence, or is required for the purpose of preventing the commission of an offence or in order to the apprehension of an offender, any person who is guilty of an offence under subsection (1) shall — ( a ) in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 6 months, or with fine which may extend to $5,000, or with both; or ( b ) in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000.

raw.marginal_note: Furnishing false information
raw.text: —(1) A person who, being legally bound to furnish information on any subject to any public servant, as such, furnishes, as true, information on the subject which the person knows or has reason to believe to be false, shall — ( a ) in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 6 months, or with fine which may extend to $5,000, or with both; or ( b ) in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000. (2) If the information which the person mentioned in subsection (1) is legally bound to furnish respects the commission of an offence, or is required for the purpose of preventing the commission of an offence, or in order to the apprehension of an offender, the person who is guilty of an offence under that subsection shall —

sub_items.text: —(1) A person who, being legally bound to furnish information on any subject to any public servant, as such, furnishes, as true, information on the subject which the person knows or has reason to believe to be false, shall — ( a ) in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 6 months, or with fine which may extend to $5,000, or with both; or ( b ) in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000.

sub_items.label: (a)
sub_items.text: in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 6 months, or with fine which may extend to $5,000, or with both; or

sub_items.label: (b)
sub_items.text: in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000.

(2) If the information which the person mentioned in subsection (1) is legally bound to furnish respects the commission of an offence, or is required for the purpose of preventing the commission of an offence, or in order to the apprehension of an offender, the person who is guilty of an offence under that subsection shall — ( a ) in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 3 years, or with fine, or with both; or ( b ) in any other case, be punished with fine.

sub_items.label: (2)(a)
sub_items.text: in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 3 years, or with fine, or with both; or

sub_items.label: (2)(b)
sub_items.text: in any other case, be punished with fine.
Illustration

A , a landholder, knowing of the commission of a murder, within the limits of his commercial property, intentionally misinforms the police that the death has occurred by accident in consequence of the bite of a snake. A is guilty of the offence defined in this section.

Explanation .—In this section and section 176, “offence” includes any act committed at any place out of Singapore, which if committed in Singapore would be punishable under any of the following sections, namely, 302, 304, 382, 392, 393, 394, 395, 396, 397, 399, 402, 435, 436, 449, 450, 459 and 460, and “offender” includes any person who is alleged to have been guilty of any such act.

178.section_title: Refusing oath when duly required to take oath by a public servant

raw.marginal_note: Refusing oath when duly required to take oath by a public servant
raw.text: Whoever refuses to bind himself by an oath to state the truth, when required so to bind himself by a public servant legally competent to require that he shall so bind himself, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 6 months, or with fine which may extend to $5,000, or with both.

181.section_title: False statement on oath to public servant or person authorised to administer an oath

raw.marginal_note: False statement on oath to public servant or person authorised to administer an oath
raw.text: Whoever, being legally bound by an oath to state the truth on any subject to any public servant or other person authorised by law to administer such oaths, makes to such public servant or other person as aforesaid, touching that subject, any statement which is false, and which he either knows or believes to be false or does not believe to be true, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 3 years, and shall also be liable to fine.

elements_detail.label: legal_oath
elements_detail.text: Being legally bound by an oath to state the truth on any subject

elements_detail.label: statement_to_authorised_person
elements_detail.text: Making to any public servant or other person authorised by law to administer such oaths, touching that subject, a statement which is false

elements_detail.label: falsity_knowledge
elements_detail.text: Knowing or believing that statement to be false, or not believing it to be true

SECTION 181: False statement on oath to public servant or person authorised to administer an oath
============================================================
Jurisdiction: singapore
  act: Penal Code 1871
  source: https://sso.agc.gov.sg/Act/PC1871?ProvIds=pr181-#pr181-
Effective: 1872-01-01

Elements of the offence:
  Conjunctive requirement (all_of): every following limb must be proved:
    Circumstance: Being legally bound by an oath to state the truth on any subject
    Physical element (actus reus): Making to any public servant or other person authorised by law to administer such oaths, touching that subject, a statement which is false
    Mental element (mens rea): Knowing or believing that statement to be false, or not believing it to be true

Penalty:
  Shall be punished with imprisonment for a term of not less than no time and not more than 3 years.

metadata.summary: A person who is legally bound by oath to tell the truth commits an offence by making a false statement on that subject to an authorised oath-taker while knowing or believing it is false or not true.

182.section_title: False information, with intent to cause a public servant to use his lawful power to the injury of another person

raw.marginal_note: False information, with intent to cause a public servant to use his lawful power to the injury of another person
raw.text: Whoever gives to any public servant any information which he knows or believes to be false, intending thereby to cause, or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby cause, such public servant to use the lawful power of such public servant to the injury or annoyance of any person, or to do or omit anything which such public servant ought not to, or would not, do or omit if the true state of facts respecting which such information is given were known by him, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 2 years, or with fine, or with both.

sub_items.text: Illustrations

sub_items.label: a
sub_items.text: ( a ) A informs a superintendent of police that Z , a police officer subordinate to such superintendent, has been guilty of neglect of duty or misconduct, knowing such information to be false, and knowing it to be likely that the information will cause the superintendent to dismiss Z . A has committed the offence defined in this section.

sub_items.label: b
sub_items.text: ( b ) A falsely informs a public servant that Z has contraband opium in a secret place, knowing such information to be false, and knowing that it is likely that the consequence of the information will be a search of Z ’s premises, attended with annoyance to Z. A has committed the offence defined in this section.

sub_items.label: c
sub_items.text: ( c ) A falsely informs a policeman that he has been assaulted and robbed by a person whose identity he does not know. A also mentions that he often sees that person going in and out of a block of flats, knowing it to be likely that in consequence of this information, the police will make inquiries and institute searches in the block of flats to the annoyance of the flat dwellers or some of them. A has committed an offence under this section.

sub_items.label: d
sub_items.text: ( d ) A informs a policeman that A does not know the identity of the suspect of a criminal offence, knowing such information to be false, and knowing it to be likely that in consequence of this information, the policeman will expend additional time and resources to identify the suspect which will not be so expended if A had informed the policeman that A knew the identity of the suspect. A has committed an offence under this section.

elements_detail.label: giving_information
elements_detail.text: Giving to any public servant any information.

elements_detail.label: falsity
elements_detail.text: Knowing or believing the information to be false.

elements_detail.label: intends_consequence
elements_detail.text: Intending thereby to cause such public servant to use the lawful power of such public servant.

elements_detail.label: knows_likely_consequence
elements_detail.text: Knowing it to be likely that he will thereby cause such public servant to use the lawful power of such public servant.

elements_detail.label: injury_or_annoyance
elements_detail.text: Such public servant uses the lawful power of such public servant to the injury or annoyance of any person.

elements_detail.label: improper_action_or_omission
elements_detail.text: Such public servant does or omits anything which such public servant ought not to, or would not, do or omit if the true state of facts respecting which such information is given were known by him.

183.section_title: Resistance to taking of property by lawful authority of public servant

raw.marginal_note: Resistance to taking of property by lawful authority of public servant
raw.text: A person who offers any resistance to the taking of any property by the lawful authority of any public servant, knowing or having reason to believe that the person is such public servant, shall — ( a ) in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 6 months, or with fine which may extend to $5,000, or with both; or ( b ) in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000.

sub_items.text: in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 6 months, or with fine which may extend to $5,000, or with both; or

184.section_title: Obstructing sale of property offered for sale by authority of public servant

raw.marginal_note: Obstructing sale of property offered for sale by authority of public servant

185.section_title: Illegal purchase or bid for property offered for sale by authority of public servant

raw.marginal_note: Illegal purchase or bid for property offered for sale by authority of public servant
raw.text: A person who, at any sale of property held by the lawful authority of a public servant as such, purchases or bids for any property on account of the person or another person, whom the person knows to be under a legal incapacity to purchase that property at that sale, or bids for such property not intending to perform the obligations under which the person lays himself by such bidding, shall — ( a ) in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to $1,500, or with both; or ( b ) in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000.

186.section_title: Obstructing public servant in discharge of his public functions

raw.marginal_note: Obstructing public servant in discharge of his public functions
raw.text: —(1) A person who voluntarily obstructs any public servant in the discharge of the public servant’s public functions, shall — ( a ) in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 6 months, or with fine which may extend to $2,500, or with both; or ( b ) in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000. (2) For the purposes of this section, an obstruction may be caused other than by the use of physical means or threatening language by a person against a public servant.

sub_items.text: —(1) A person who voluntarily obstructs any public servant in the discharge of the public servant’s public functions, shall — ( a ) in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 6 months, or with fine which may extend to $2,500, or with both; or ( b ) in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000.

sub_items.label: (a)
sub_items.text: in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 6 months, or with fine which may extend to $2,500, or with both; or

sub_items.label: (b)
sub_items.text: in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000.
(2) For the purposes of this section, an obstruction may be caused other than by the use of physical means or threatening language by a person against a public servant.
Illustration

A group of paramedics from the Singapore Civil Defence Force intends to take the lift to the 30th storey of a block of flats to reach a person suffering from a heart attack. A decides to play a prank on the paramedics by telling them that the lift has broken down, knowing this to be false. The paramedics took the stairs because of what A told them and reached the person 15 minutes later than if they had taken the lift. A has voluntarily obstructed the paramedics in the discharge of their public function.

187.section_title: Omission to assist public servant when bound by law to give assistance

raw.marginal_note: Omission to assist public servant when bound by law to give assistance
raw.text: —(1) A person who, being bound by law to render or furnish assistance to any public servant in the execution of the public servant’s public duty, intentionally omits to give such assistance, shall — ( a ) in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to $1,500, or with both; or ( b ) in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000. (2) If such assistance is demanded of the person mentioned in subsection (1) by a public servant legally competent to make such demand for the purposes of executing any process lawfully issued by a court, or of preventing the commission of an offence, or of suppressing a riot or an affray, or of apprehending a person charged with or guilty of an offence, or of having escaped from lawful custody, the person who is guilty of an offence under that subsection shall —

sub_items.text: —(1) A person who, being bound by law to render or furnish assistance to any public servant in the execution of the public servant’s public duty, intentionally omits to give such assistance, shall — ( a ) in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to $1,500, or with both; or ( b ) in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000.

sub_items.label: (a)
sub_items.text: in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to $1,500, or with both; or

sub_items.label: (b)
sub_items.text: in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000.

(2) If such assistance is demanded of the person mentioned in subsection (1) by a public servant legally competent to make such demand for the purposes of executing any process lawfully issued by a court, or of preventing the commission of an offence, or of suppressing a riot or an affray, or of apprehending a person charged with or guilty of an offence, or of having escaped from lawful custody, the person who is guilty of an offence under that subsection shall — ( a ) in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 6 months, or with fine which may extend to $5,000, or with both; or ( b ) in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000.

sub_items.label: (2)(a)
sub_items.text: in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 6 months, or with fine which may extend to $5,000, or with both; or

sub_items.label: (2)(b)

188.section_title: Disobedience to order duly promulgated by public servant

raw.marginal_note: Disobedience to order duly promulgated by public servant
raw.text: —(1) A person who, knowing that by an order promulgated by a public servant lawfully empowered to promulgate such order the person is directed to abstain from a certain act, or to take certain order with certain property in the person’s possession or under the person’s management, disobeys such direction, shall, if such disobedience causes or tends to cause obstruction, annoyance or injury, or risk of obstruction, annoyance or injury, to any person lawfully employed — ( a ) in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to $1,500, or with both; or ( b ) in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000. (2) If such disobedience mentioned in subsection (1) causes or tends to cause danger to human life, health or safety, or causes or tends to cause a riot or an affray, the person who is guilty of an offence under that subsection shall —

sub_items.text: —(1) A person who, knowing that by an order promulgated by a public servant lawfully empowered to promulgate such order the person is directed to abstain from a certain act, or to take certain order with certain property in the person’s possession or under the person’s management, disobeys such direction, shall, if such disobedience causes or tends to cause obstruction, annoyance or injury, or risk of obstruction, annoyance or injury, to any person lawfully employed — ( a ) in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to $1,500, or with both; or ( b ) in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000.

sub_items.label: (a)
sub_items.text: in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to $1,500, or with both; or

sub_items.label: (b)
sub_items.text: in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000.

(2) If such disobedience mentioned in subsection (1) causes or tends to cause danger to human life, health or safety, or causes or tends to cause a riot or an affray, the person who is guilty of an offence under that subsection shall — ( a ) in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 6 months, or with fine which may extend to $5,000, or with both; or ( b ) in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000.

sub_items.label: (2)(a)
sub_items.text: in the case of an individual, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 6 months, or with fine which may extend to $5,000, or with both; or

sub_items.label: (2)(b)
sub_items.text: in any other case, be punished with fine which may extend to $10,000.

Explanation .—It is not necessary that the offender should intend to produce harm, or contemplate his disobedience as likely to produce harm. It is sufficient that he knows of the order which he disobeys, and that his disobedience produces, or is likely to produce, harm.
Illustration

An order is promulgated by a public servant lawfully empowered to promulgate such order, directing that a religious procession must not pass down a certain street. A knowingly disobeys the order, and thereby causes danger of riot. A has committed the offence defined in this section.

A knowingly disobeys the order, and thereby causes danger of riot. A has committed the offence defined in this section.
  Provenance: statutory illustration attached to Section 188.

metadata.summary: Section 188 criminalises knowingly disobeying a duly promulgated public-servant order when the disobedience causes specified harm or risk, with higher penalties for danger to life, health, safety, riot or affray.

189.section_title: Threat of injury to a public servant

raw.marginal_note: Threat of injury to a public servant
raw.text: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant, or to any person in whom he believes that public servant to be interested, for the purpose of inducing that public servant to do any act, or to forbear or delay to do any act, connected with the exercise of the public functions of such public servant, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 2 years, or with fine, or with both.

elements_detail.label: threat
elements_detail.text: Holds out any threat of injury

elements_detail.label: target_public_servant
elements_detail.text: To any public servant

elements_detail.label: target_interested_person
elements_detail.text: To any person in whom he believes that public servant to be interested

elements_detail.label: purpose
elements_detail.text: For the purpose of inducing that public servant

elements_detail.label: induce_do_act
elements_detail.text: To do any act connected with the exercise of the public functions of such public servant

elements_detail.label: induce_forbear_or_delay
elements_detail.text: To forbear or delay to do any act connected with the exercise of the public functions of such public servant

raw.marginal_note: “Judge”
raw.text: The word “judge” denotes not only every person who is officially designated as a judge, but also every person who is empowered by law to give, in any legal proceeding, civil or criminal, a definitive judgment, or a judgment which, if not appealed against, would be definitive, or a judgment which, if confirmed by some other authority, would be definitive, or who is one of a body of persons, which body of persons is empowered by law to give such a judgment.

sub_items.text: Illustrations

sub_items.label: a
sub_items.text: ( a ) A Magistrate exercising jurisdiction in respect of a charge on which he has power to sentence to fine or imprisonment, with or without appeal, is a judge.

sub_items.label: b
sub_items.text: ( b ) Officers holding an inquiry as to the loss of a ship under the Merchant Shipping Act 1995 are judges.

sub_items.label: c
sub_items.text: ( c ) A Magistrate exercising jurisdiction in respect of a charge on which he has power only to commit for trial to another court, is not a judge.

190.section_title: Threat of injury to induce any person to refrain from applying for protection to a public servant

raw.marginal_note: Threat of injury to induce any person to refrain from applying for protection to a public servant
raw.text: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any person for the purpose of inducing that person to refrain or desist from making a legal application, for protection against any injury, to any public servant legally empowered as such to give such protection or to cause such protection to be given, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both.

elements_detail.label: protection_application

raw.marginal_note: Giving false evidence
raw.text: Whoever, being legally bound by an oath, or by any express provision of law to state the truth, or being bound by law to make a declaration upon any subject, makes any statement which is false, and which he either knows or believes to be false, or does not believe to be true, is said to give false evidence.

sub_items.text: Explanation 1.—A statement is within the meaning of this section whether it is made verbally or otherwise.

Explanation 2.—A false statement as to the belief of the person attesting is within the meaning of this section, and a person may be guilty of giving false evidence by stating that he believes a thing which he does not believe, as well as by stating that he knows a thing which he does not know.
Illustrations

sub_items.label: a
sub_items.text: ( a ) A , in support of a just claim which B has against Z for $1,000, falsely swears on a trial that he heard Z admit the justice of B ’s claim. A has given false evidence.

sub_items.label: b
sub_items.text: ( b ) A , being bound by an oath to state the truth, states that he believes a certain signature to be the handwriting of Z , when he does not believe it to be the handwriting of Z . Here A states that which he knows to be false, and therefore gives false evidence.

sub_items.label: c
sub_items.text: ( c ) A , knowing the general character of Z ’s handwriting, states that he believes a certain signature to be the handwriting of Z , A in good faith believing it to be so. Here A ’s statement is merely as to his belief, and is true as to his belief, and therefore, although the signature may not be the handwriting of Z , A has not given false evidence.

sub_items.label: d
sub_items.text: ( d ) A , being bound by an oath to state the truth, states that he knows that Z was at a particular place on a particular day, not knowing anything upon the subject. A gives false evidence as to whether Z was at that place on the day named, or not.

sub_items.label: e
sub_items.text: ( e ) A , an interpreter or a translator, gives or certifies as a true interpretation or translation of a statement or document which he is bound by oath to interpret or translate truly, that which is not and which he does not believe to be a true interpretation or translation. A has given false evidence.

elements_detail.label: legal_obligation
elements_detail.text: The person is legally bound by an oath, or by an express provision of law to state the truth, or is bound by law to make a declaration upon any subject.

elements_detail.label: false_statement
elements_detail.text: The person makes a statement which is false.

elements_detail.label: knows_false
elements_detail.text: The person knows the statement to be false.

elements_detail.label: believes_false
elements_detail.text: The person believes the statement to be false.

elements_detail.label: does_not_believe_true
elements_detail.text: The person does not believe the statement to be true.

Definitions:
  "giving_false_evidence" means Whoever, being legally bound by an oath, or by any express provision of law to state the truth, or being bound by law to make a declaration upon any subject, makes any statement which is false, and which he either knows or believes to be false, or does not believe to be true, is said to give false evidence.

Illustration illustration_e: ( e ) A , an interpreter or a translator, gives or certifies as a true interpretation or translation of a statement or document which he is bound by oath to interpret or translate truly, that which is not and which he does not believe to be a true interpretation or translation. A has given false evidence.

metadata.summary: Defines the offence of giving false evidence where a legally bound person makes a false statement knowing or believing it to be false, or not believing it to be true.

raw.marginal_note: Fabricating false evidence
raw.text: Whoever causes any circumstance to exist, or makes any false entry in any book or record or electronic record, or makes any document or electronic record containing a false statement, intending that such circumstance, false entry, or false statement may appear in evidence in a judicial proceeding, or in a proceeding taken by law before a public servant as such, or before an arbitrator, and that such circumstance, false entry, or false statement, so appearing in evidence, may cause any person, who in such proceeding is to form an opinion upon the evidence, to entertain an erroneous opinion touching any point material to the result of such proceeding, is said “to fabricate false evidence”.

sub_items.text: Illustrations

sub_items.label: a
sub_items.text: ( a ) A puts jewels into a box belonging to Z with the intention that they may be found in that box and that this circumstance may cause Z to be convicted of theft. A has fabricated false evidence.

sub_items.label: b
sub_items.text: ( b ) A makes a false entry in his shop‑book for the purpose of using it as corroborative evidence in a court of justice. A has fabricated false evidence.

sub_items.label: c
sub_items.text: ( c ) A , with the intention of causing Z to be convicted of a criminal conspiracy, writes a letter in imitation of Z ’s handwriting, purporting to be addressed to an accomplice in such criminal conspiracy, and puts the letter in a place which he knows that the officers of the police are likely to search. A has fabricated false evidence.

elements_detail.label: fabrication
elements_detail.text: Causes any circumstance to exist, or makes any false entry in any book or record or electronic record, or makes any document or electronic record containing a false statement.

elements_detail.label: intended_evidential_appearance
elements_detail.text: Such circumstance, false entry, or false statement may appear in evidence in a judicial proceeding, or in a proceeding taken by law before a public servant as such, or before an arbitrator.

elements_detail.label: intended_erroneous_opinion
elements_detail.text: Intends that such circumstance, false entry, or false statement, so appearing in evidence, may cause any person, who in such proceeding is to form an opinion upon the evidence, to entertain an erroneous opinion touching any point material to the result of such proceeding.

metadata.summary: Defines when a person fabricates false evidence by creating false material intended to appear in evidence and mislead the decision-maker on a material point.

raw.marginal_note: Punishment for false evidence
raw.text: Whoever intentionally gives false evidence in any stage of a judicial proceeding, or fabricates false evidence for the purpose of being used in any stage of a judicial proceeding, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 7 years, and shall also be liable to fine; and whoever intentionally gives or fabricates false evidence in any other case, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 3 years, and shall also be liable to fine.

sub_items.text: Explanation 1.—A trial before a court martial is a judicial proceeding.

Explanation 2.—An investigation directed by law preliminary to a proceeding before a court of justice, is a stage of a judicial proceeding, though that investigation may not take place before a court of justice.
Illustration

A , in an inquiry before a Magistrate for the purpose of ascertaining whether Z ought to be committed for trial, makes on oath a statement which he knows to be false. As this inquiry is a stage of a judicial proceeding, A has given false evidence.

Explanation 3.—An investigation directed by a court of justice according to law, and conducted under the authority of a court of justice, is a stage of a judicial proceeding, though that investigation may not take place before a court of justice.
Illustration

A , in an inquiry before an officer deputed by a court of justice to ascertain on the spot the boundaries of land, makes on oath a statement which he knows to be false. As this inquiry is a stage of a judicial proceeding, A has given false evidence.

194.section_title: Giving or fabricating false evidence with intent to procure conviction of a capital offence

raw.marginal_note: Giving or fabricating false evidence with intent to procure conviction of a capital offence
raw.text: Whoever gives or fabricates false evidence, intending thereby to cause, or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby cause, any person to be convicted of an offence which is capital by this Code, or under any other law for the time being in force, shall be punished with imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 20 years, and shall, if he is not sentenced to imprisonment for life, also be liable to fine; and if an innocent person is convicted and executed in consequence of such false evidence, the person who gives such false evidence shall be punished either with death or the punishment hereinbefore described.

elements_detail.label: gives_false_evidence
elements_detail.text: Gives false evidence

elements_detail.label: fabricates_false_evidence
elements_detail.text: Fabricates false evidence

elements_detail.text: Intending thereby to cause any person to be convicted of a capital offence

elements_detail.label: knowledge_likely_to_cause_conviction
elements_detail.text: Knowing it to be likely that he will thereby cause any person to be convicted of a capital offence

Effective: 1872-01-01

  "capital_offence" means An offence which is capital by this Code, or under any other law for the time being in force
  "aggravated_giving_false_evidence" means An innocent person is convicted and executed in consequence of such false evidence, the person who gives such false evidence shall be punished either with death or the punishment hereinbefore described.

  Shall be punished with imprisonment for a term of not less than no time and not more than 20 years, or with death, or with any combination thereof.

  Additionally: If an innocent person is convicted and executed in consequence of such false evidence, the person who gives such false evidence shall be punished either with death or the punishment hereinbefore described. [15/2019]

metadata.summary: Section 194 criminalises giving or fabricating false evidence with intent or knowledge that it is likely to procure another person's conviction for a capital offence, with an aggravated death-eligible branch if an innocent person is convicted and executed.

195.section_title: Giving or fabricating false evidence with intent to procure conviction of an offence punishable with imprisonment

raw.marginal_note: Giving or fabricating false evidence with intent to procure conviction of an offence punishable with imprisonment
raw.text: —(1) Whoever gives or fabricates false evidence, intending thereby to cause, or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby cause, any person to be convicted of an offence which by this Code or under any other law for the time being in force is not capital, but punishable with imprisonment for a term of 7 years or upwards, shall be punished as a person convicted of that offence would be liable to be punished. (2) Whoever gives or fabricates false evidence, intending thereby to cause, or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby cause, any person to be convicted of an offence which by this Code or under any other law for the time being in force is not capital, but punishable with imprisonment for life, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 20 years.

sub_items.text: —(1) Whoever gives or fabricates false evidence, intending thereby to cause, or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby cause, any person to be convicted of an offence which by this Code or under any other law for the time being in force is not capital, but punishable with imprisonment for a term of 7 years or upwards, shall be punished as a person convicted of that offence would be liable to be punished.

(2) Whoever gives or fabricates false evidence, intending thereby to cause, or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby cause, any person to be convicted of an offence which by this Code or under any other law for the time being in force is not capital, but punishable with imprisonment for life, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 20 years.
Illustration

A gives false evidence before a court of justice, intending thereby to cause Z to be convicted of a gang‑robbery. The punishment of gang-robbery is imprisonment for a term of not less than 5 years and not more than 20 years and also caning with not less than 12 strokes. A , therefore, is liable to such imprisonment, with caning.

  Provenance: statutory illustration attached to Section 195.

outgoing.snippet: Giving false evidence as defined in section 191.
Fabricating false evidence as defined in section 192.

metadata.summary: Giving or fabricating false evidence to procure conviction for a serious non-capital imprisonable offence attracts either the predicate offence's punishment or up to 20 years' imprisonment, depending on the target offence.

196.section_title: Using evidence known to be false

raw.marginal_note: Using evidence known to be false
raw.text: Whoever corruptly uses or attempts to use as true or genuine evidence any evidence which he knows to be false or fabricated, shall be punished in the same manner as if he gave or fabricated false evidence.

199.section_title: False statement made in any declaration which is by law receivable as evidence

raw.marginal_note: False statement made in any declaration which is by law receivable as evidence
raw.text: Whoever, in any declaration made or subscribed by him, which declaration any court of justice, or any public servant or other person, is bound or authorised by law to receive as evidence of any fact, makes any statement which is false, and which he either knows or believes to be false or does not believe to be true, touching any point material to the object for which the declaration is made or used, shall be punished in the same manner as if he gave false evidence.

2.section_title: Punishment of offences committed within Singapore

raw.marginal_note: Punishment of offences committed within Singapore
raw.text: Every person shall be liable to punishment under this Code and not otherwise for every act or omission contrary to the provisions thereof, of which he is guilty within Singapore.

201.section_title: Causing disappearance of evidence of an offence committed, or giving false information touching it, to screen the offender

raw.marginal_note: Causing disappearance of evidence of an offence committed, or giving false information touching it, to screen the offender
raw.text: Whoever, knowing or having reason to believe that an offence has been committed, causes any evidence of the commission of that offence to disappear with the intention of screening the offender from legal punishment, or with that intention gives any information respecting the offence which he knows or believes to be false, shall — ( a ) if the offence which he knows or believes to have been committed is punishable with death, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 10 years, and shall also be liable to fine; ( b ) if the offence is punishable with imprisonment for life or with imprisonment which may extend to 20 years, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 7 years, and shall also be liable to fine; and ( c ) if the offence is punishable with imprisonment for any term not extending to 20 years, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one‑fourth part of the longest term of the imprisonment provided for the offence, or with fine, or with both.

sub_items.label: (a)
sub_items.text: if the offence which he knows or believes to have been committed is punishable with death, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 10 years, and shall also be liable to fine;

sub_items.label: (b)
sub_items.text: if the offence is punishable with imprisonment for life or with imprisonment which may extend to 20 years, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 7 years, and shall also be liable to fine; and

sub_items.label: (c)
sub_items.text: if the offence is punishable with imprisonment for any term not extending to 20 years, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one‑fourth part of the longest term of the imprisonment provided for the offence, or with fine, or with both.
Illustration
A , knowing that B has murdered Z , assists B to hide the body with the intention of screening B from punishment. A is liable to imprisonment for 10 years, and also to fine.

elements_detail.label: knowledge_of_offence
elements_detail.text: Knowing or having reason to believe that an offence has been committed

elements_detail.label: intention_to_screen
elements_detail.text: With the intention of screening the offender from legal punishment

elements_detail.label: disappearance_of_evidence
elements_detail.text: Causes any evidence of the commission of that offence to disappear

elements_detail.label: false_information
elements_detail.text: Gives any information respecting the offence which he knows or believes to be false

elements_detail.label: death_punishable_offence
elements_detail.text: The offence which he knows or believes to have been committed is punishable with death

elements_detail.label: life_or_20_year_offence
elements_detail.text: The offence is punishable with imprisonment for life or with imprisonment which may extend to 20 years

elements_detail.label: below_20_year_offence
elements_detail.text: The offence is punishable with imprisonment for any term not extending to 20 years

    Mental element (mens rea): Knowing or having reason to believe that an offence has been committed
    Mental element (mens rea): With the intention of screening the offender from legal punishment
    Alternative requirement (any_of): proving one following limb is enough:

      Circumstance: The offence is punishable with imprisonment for any term not extending to 20 years

204.section_title: Destruction of document or electronic record to prevent its production as evidence

raw.marginal_note: Destruction of document or electronic record to prevent its production as evidence
raw.text: Whoever secretes or destroys any document or electronic record which he may be lawfully compelled to produce as evidence before a court of justice, or in any proceeding lawfully held before a public servant as such, or obliterates or renders illegible the whole or any part of such document or electronic record with the intention of preventing the same from being produced or used as evidence before such court or public servant as aforesaid, or after he has been lawfully summoned or required to produce the same for that purpose, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 2 years, or with fine, or with both.

204A.section_title: Obstructing, preventing, perverting or defeating course of justice

raw.marginal_note: Obstructing, preventing, perverting or defeating course of justice
raw.text: Whoever does an act that has a tendency to obstruct, prevent, pervert or defeat the course of justice — ( a ) knowing that the act is likely to obstruct, prevent, pervert or defeat the course of justice; or ( b ) intending to obstruct, prevent, pervert or defeat the course of justice, shall be guilty of an offence and shall on conviction be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 7 years, or with fine, or with both.

raw.marginal_note: Bribery of witnesses
raw.text: —(1) Whoever — ( a ) gives, confers, or procures, promises or offers to give, confer, or procure or attempts to procure, any gratification to, upon, or for any person, upon any agreement or understanding that any person who is aware of any offence (being an offence which any person is legally bound to give information respecting that offence) will abstain from reporting that offence to the police or any agency charged by law with the duty of investigating offences; ( b ) gives, confers, or procures, promises or offers to give, confer, or procure or attempts to procure, any gratification to, upon, or for any person, upon any agreement or understanding that any person called or to be called as a witness in any judicial proceeding will give false testimony or withhold true testimony or abstain from giving evidence; ( c ) attempts by any means to induce a person called or to be called as a witness in any judicial proceeding to give false testimony or withhold true testimony or abstain from giving evidence; or ( d ) asks, receives, or obtains, or agrees or attempts to receive or obtain, any property or benefit of any kind for himself, or any other person, upon any agreement or understanding that any person will as a witness in any judicial proceeding give false testimony or withhold true testimony or abstain from giving evidence, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 7 years, or with fine, or with both. (2) In this section, “judicial proceeding” means any proceeding in the course of which evidence is or may be legally taken.

sub_items.text: —(1) Whoever — ( a ) gives, confers, or procures, promises or offers to give, confer, or procure or attempts to procure, any gratification to, upon, or for any person, upon any agreement or understanding that any person who is aware of any offence (being an offence which any person is legally bound to give information respecting that offence) will abstain from reporting that offence to the police or any agency charged by law with the duty of investigating offences; ( b ) gives, confers, or procures, promises or offers to give, confer, or procure or attempts to procure, any gratification to, upon, or for any person, upon any agreement or understanding that any person called or to be called as a witness in any judicial proceeding will give false testimony or withhold true testimony or abstain from giving evidence; ( c ) attempts by any means to induce a person called or to be called as a witness in any judicial proceeding to give false testimony or withhold true testimony or abstain from giving evidence; or ( d ) asks, receives, or obtains, or agrees or attempts to receive or obtain, any property or benefit of any kind for himself, or any other person, upon any agreement or understanding that any person will as a witness in any judicial proceeding give false testimony or withhold true testimony or abstain from giving evidence, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 7 years, or with fine, or with both.

206.section_title: Fraudulent removal or concealment of property to prevent its seizure as a forfeiture, in execution of a decree or under or pursuant to an enforcement order

raw.marginal_note: Fraudulent removal or concealment of property to prevent its seizure as a forfeiture, in execution of a decree or under or pursuant to an enforcement order
raw.text: Whoever fraudulently removes, conceals, transfers, or delivers to any person any property or any interest therein, intending thereby to prevent that property or interest therein from being taken as a forfeiture or in satisfaction of a fine, under a sentence which has been pronounced, or which he knows to be likely to be pronounced by a court of justice or other competent authority, or from being taken in execution of a decree or an order, or under or pursuant to an enforcement order, which has been made, or which he knows to be likely to be made by a court of justice in a civil suit, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 2 years, or with fine, or with both.

elements_detail.label: dealing_with_property
elements_detail.text: Whoever fraudulently removes, conceals, transfers, or delivers to any person any property or any interest therein

elements_detail.label: intention_to_prevent_seizure
elements_detail.text: Intending thereby to prevent that property or interest therein from being taken

elements_detail.label: forfeiture_or_fine_under_sentence
elements_detail.text: As a forfeiture or in satisfaction of a fine, under a sentence which has been pronounced, or which he knows to be likely to be pronounced by a court of justice or other competent authority

elements_detail.label: execution_or_enforcement
elements_detail.text: In execution of a decree or an order, or under or pursuant to an enforcement order, which has been made, or which he knows to be likely to be made by a court of justice in a civil suit

207.section_title: Fraudulent claim to property to prevent its seizure as a forfeiture, in execution of a decree or under or pursuant to an enforcement order

raw.marginal_note: Fraudulent claim to property to prevent its seizure as a forfeiture, in execution of a decree or under or pursuant to an enforcement order
raw.text: Whoever fraudulently accepts, receives or claims any property or any interest therein, knowing that he has no right or rightful claim to such property or interest, or practises any deception touching any right to any property or any interest therein, intending thereby to prevent that property or interest therein from being taken as a forfeiture or in satisfaction of a fine under a sentence which has been pronounced, or which he knows to be likely to be pronounced by a court of justice or other competent authority, or from being taken in execution of a decree or an order, or under or pursuant to an enforcement order, which has been made or which he knows to be likely to be made by a court of justice in a civil suit, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 2 years, or with fine, or with both.

208.section_title: Fraudulently suffering a decree for a sum not due

raw.marginal_note: Fraudulently suffering a decree for a sum not due
raw.text: Whoever fraudulently causes or suffers a decree or an order to be passed against him at the suit of any person for a sum not due, or for a larger sum than is due to such person, or for any property or interest in property to which such person is not entitled, or fraudulently causes or suffers a decree or an order to be executed or enforced against him after it has been satisfied, or for anything in respect of which it has been satisfied, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 2 years, or with fine, or with both.

sub_items.text: Illustration

A institutes a suit against Z . Z , knowing that A is likely to obtain a decree against him, fraudulently suffers a judgment to pass against him for a larger amount at the suit of B , who has no just claim against him, in order that B , either on his own account or for the benefit of Z , may share in the proceeds of any sale of Z ’s property which may be made under A ’s decree. Z has committed an offence under this section.

elements_detail.label: fraudulently
elements_detail.text: Fraudulently

elements_detail.label: sum_not_due
elements_detail.text: Causes or suffers a decree or an order to be passed against him at the suit of any person for a sum not due

elements_detail.label: larger_sum_than_due
elements_detail.text: Causes or suffers a decree or an order to be passed against him at the suit of any person for a larger sum than is due to such person

elements_detail.label: property_not_entitled
elements_detail.text: Causes or suffers a decree or an order to be passed against him at the suit of any person for any property or interest in property to which such person is not entitled

elements_detail.label: executed_after_satisfied
elements_detail.text: Causes or suffers a decree or an order to be executed or enforced against him after it has been satisfied

elements_detail.label: executed_for_satisfied_matter
elements_detail.text: Causes or suffers a decree or an order to be executed or enforced against him for anything in respect of which it has been satisfied

raw.marginal_note: “Public servant”
raw.text: —(1) The words “public servant” denote a person falling under any of the following descriptions: ( a ) every officer in the Singapore Armed Forces; ( b ) every judge; ( c ) every officer of a court of justice whose duty it is, as such officer, to investigate or report on any matter of law or fact, or to make, authenticate, or keep any document, or to take charge or dispose of any property, or to execute any judicial process, or to administer any oath, or to interpret, or to preserve order in the court, or to conduct mediation or other alternative dispute resolution process under any written law, and every person specially authorised by a court of justice to perform any of such duties; ( d ) every assessor assisting a court of justice or public servant; ( e ) every arbitrator or other person to whom any cause or matter has been referred for decision or report by any court of justice, or by any other competent public authority; ( f ) every person who holds any office by virtue of which he is empowered to place or keep any person in confinement; ( g ) every officer of the Government and every officer or employee of a body corporate established by a public Act for the purposes of a public function whose duty, as such officer or employee, is any of the following: ( h ) every officer whose duty it is, as such officer, to take, receive, keep or expend any property, on behalf of Government, or to make any survey, assessment, or contract on behalf of Government, or to execute any revenue process, or to investigate, or to report on any matter affecting the pecuniary interests of Government, or to make, authenticate or keep any document relating to the pecuniary interests of Government, or to prevent the infraction of any law for the protection of the pecuniary interests of Government, and every officer in the service or pay of Government, or remunerated by fees or commission for the performance of any public duty; ( i ) a member of the Public Service Commission, the Judicial Service Commission or the Legal Service Commission; ( j ) every person (other than a person mentioned in paragraph ( g )) who is employed to carry out any of the duties, mentioned in paragraph ( g )(i) to (vii), on behalf of the Government or a body corporate established by a public Act for the purposes of a public function, when that person is performing such duties. (2) Despite subsection (1), the words “public servant” in sections 175, 178, 179, 180 and 228 does not include a judge as defined in the Administration of Justice (Protection) Act 2016.

sub_items.text: —(1) The words “public servant” denote a person falling under any of the following descriptions: ( a ) every officer in the Singapore Armed Forces; ( b ) every judge; ( c ) every officer of a court of justice whose duty it is, as such officer, to investigate or report on any matter of law or fact, or to make, authenticate, or keep any document, or to take charge or dispose of any property, or to execute any judicial process, or to administer any oath, or to interpret, or to preserve order in the court, or to conduct mediation or other alternative dispute resolution process under any written law, and every person specially authorised by a court of justice to perform any of such duties; ( d ) every assessor assisting a court of justice or public servant; ( e ) every arbitrator or other person to whom any cause or matter has been referred for decision or report by any court of justice, or by any other competent public authority; ( f ) every person who holds any office by virtue of which he is empowered to place or keep any person in confinement; ( g ) every officer of the Government and every officer or employee of a body corporate established by a public Act for the purposes of a public function whose duty, as such officer or employee, is any of the following: ( h ) every officer whose duty it is, as such officer, to take, receive, keep or expend any property, on behalf of Government, or to make any survey, assessment, or contract on behalf of Government, or to execute any revenue process, or to investigate, or to report on any matter affecting the pecuniary interests of Government, or to make, authenticate or keep any document relating to the pecuniary interests of Government, or to prevent the infraction of any law for the protection of the pecuniary interests of Government, and every officer in the service or pay of Government, or remunerated by fees or commission for the performance of any public duty; ( i ) a member of the Public Service Commission, the Judicial Service Commission or the Legal Service Commission; ( j ) every person (other than a person mentioned in paragraph ( g )) who is employed to carry out any of the duties, mentioned in paragraph ( g )(i) to (vii), on behalf of the Government or a body corporate established by a public Act for the purposes of a public function, when that person is performing such duties.

sub_items.label: (a)
sub_items.text: every officer in the Singapore Armed Forces;

sub_items.label: (b)
sub_items.text: every judge;

sub_items.label: (c)
sub_items.text: every officer of a court of justice whose duty it is, as such officer, to investigate or report on any matter of law or fact, or to make, authenticate, or keep any document, or to take charge or dispose of any property, or to execute any judicial process, or to administer any oath, or to interpret, or to preserve order in the court, or to conduct mediation or other alternative dispute resolution process under any written law, and every person specially authorised by a court of justice to perform any of such duties;

sub_items.label: (d)
sub_items.text: every assessor assisting a court of justice or public servant;

sub_items.label: (e)
sub_items.text: every arbitrator or other person to whom any cause or matter has been referred for decision or report by any court of justice, or by any other competent public authority;

sub_items.label: (f)
sub_items.text: every person who holds any office by virtue of which he is empowered to place or keep any person in confinement;

sub_items.label: (g)
sub_items.text: every officer of the Government and every officer or employee of a body corporate established by a public Act for the purposes of a public function whose duty, as such officer or employee, is any of the following:

sub_items.label: (g)(i)
sub_items.text: maintaining law and order;

sub_items.label: (g)(ii)
sub_items.text: preserving the public peace;

sub_items.label: (g)(iii)
sub_items.text: preventing and detecting offences;

sub_items.label: (g)(iv)
sub_items.text: apprehending offenders or otherwise bringing offenders to justice;

sub_items.label: (g)(v)
sub_items.text: executing summonses, subpoenas, orders to attend court, warrants, commitments and other legal processes issued by a court or Justice of the Peace;

sub_items.label: (g)(vi)
sub_items.text: maintaining order in the premises of the courts of justice;

sub_items.label: (g)(vii)
sub_items.text: escorting and guarding prisoners and accused persons in remand;

sub_items.label: (g)(viii)
sub_items.text: protecting the public health or safety or prevention of public inconvenience;

sub_items.label: (h)
sub_items.text: every officer whose duty it is, as such officer, to take, receive, keep or expend any property, on behalf of Government, or to make any survey, assessment, or contract on behalf of Government, or to execute any revenue process, or to investigate, or to report on any matter affecting the pecuniary interests of Government, or to make, authenticate or keep any document relating to the pecuniary interests of Government, or to prevent the infraction of any law for the protection of the pecuniary interests of Government, and every officer in the service or pay of Government, or remunerated by fees or commission for the performance of any public duty;

sub_items.label: (i)
sub_items.text: a member of the Public Service Commission, the Judicial Service Commission or the Legal Service Commission;

sub_items.label: (j)
sub_items.text: every person (other than a person mentioned in paragraph ( g )) who is employed to carry out any of the duties, mentioned in paragraph ( g )(i) to (vii), on behalf of the Government or a body corporate established by a public Act for the purposes of a public function, when that person is performing such duties.
(2) Despite subsection (1), the words “public servant” in sections 175, 178, 179, 180 and 228 does not include a judge as defined in the Administration of Justice (Protection) Act 2016.
Explanation 1.—Persons falling under any of the above descriptions are public servants, whether appointed by the Government or not.

Explanation 2.—Wherever the words “public servant” occur, they shall be understood of every person who is in actual possession of the situation of a public servant, whatever legal defect there may be in his right to hold that situation.

211.section_title: False charge of offence made with intent to injure

raw.marginal_note: False charge of offence made with intent to injure
raw.text: Whoever, with intent to cause injury to any person, institutes or causes to be instituted any criminal proceeding against that person, or falsely charges any person with having committed an offence, knowing that there is no just or lawful ground for such proceeding or charge against that person, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 2 years, or with fine, or with both; and if such criminal proceeding be instituted on a false charge of an offence punishable with death, or imprisonment for 7 years or upwards, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 7 years and shall also be liable to fine.

elements_detail.label: intent_to_cause_injury

elements_detail.label: falsely_charges_offence
elements_detail.text: Falsely charges any person with having committed an offence

elements_detail.label: knowledge_no_just_or_lawful_ground
elements_detail.text: Knowing that there is no just or lawful ground for such proceeding or charge against that person
