  act: Penal Code 1871
  source: https://sso.agc.gov.sg/Act/PC1871?ProvIds=pr330-#pr330-
Effective: 1872-01-01

Elements of the offence:
  Conjunctive requirement (all_of): every following limb must be proved:
    Physical element (actus reus): Whoever voluntarily causes hurt
    Alternative requirement (any_of): proving one following limb is enough:
      Mental element (mens rea): For the purpose of extorting from the sufferer, or from any person interested in the sufferer, any confession or any information which may lead to the detection of an offence or misconduct
      Mental element (mens rea): For the purpose of constraining the sufferer, or any person interested in the sufferer, to restore or to cause the restoration of any property or valuable security, or to satisfy any claim or demand, or to give information which may lead to the restoration of any property or valuable security

Penalty:
  Penalty to be determined.

  Additionally: and shall also be liable to fine or to caning.

Illustrations:

Illustration illustration_a: ( a ) A , a police officer, tortures Z in order to induce Z to confess that he committed a crime. A is guilty of an offence under this section.
  Provenance: statutory illustration attached to Section 330.
Illustration illustration_b: ( b ) A , a police officer, tortures B to induce him to point out where certain stolen property is deposited. A is guilty of an offence under this section.
  Provenance: statutory illustration attached to Section 330.
Illustration illustration_c: ( c ) A , a customs officer, tortures Z in order to compel him to confess to a pretended offence against the customs laws. A is guilty of an offence under this section.
  Provenance: statutory illustration attached to Section 330.

metadata.summary: This section criminalises voluntarily causing hurt in order to extort a confession or information, or to compel restoration of property or satisfaction of a claim.

331.section_title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt to extort confession or to compel restoration of property

raw.marginal_note: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt to extort confession or to compel restoration of property
raw.text: Whoever voluntarily causes grievous hurt for the purpose of extorting from the sufferer, or from any person interested in the sufferer, any confession or any information which may lead to the detection of an offence or misconduct, or for the purpose of constraining the sufferer, or any person interested in the sufferer, to restore or to cause the restoration of any property or valuable security, or to satisfy any claim or demand, or to give information which may lead to the restoration of any property or valuable security, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 10 years, and shall also be liable to fine or to caning.

332.section_title: Voluntarily causing hurt to deter public servant from his duty

raw.marginal_note: Voluntarily causing hurt to deter public servant from his duty
raw.text: Whoever voluntarily causes hurt to any person being a public servant in the discharge of his duty as such public servant, or with intent to prevent or deter that person or any other public servant from discharging his duty as such public servant, or in consequence of anything done or attempted to be done by that person in the lawful discharge of his duty as such public servant, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 7 years, and shall also be liable to fine or to caning, provided that in exceptional circumstances imprisonment need not be imposed.

elements_detail.label: hurt_caused_in_consequence_of_lawful_discharge
elements_detail.text: The hurt is caused in consequence of anything done or attempted to be done by that person in the lawful discharge of his duty as such public servant

[15/2019]

334A.section_title: Punishment for voluntarily causing hurt on provocation which causes grievous hurt

raw.marginal_note: Punishment for voluntarily causing hurt on provocation which causes grievous hurt
raw.text: Whoever voluntarily causes hurt on grave and sudden provocation, if he neither intends nor knows himself to be likely to cause hurt to any person other than the person who gave the provocation and if the hurt which he intends to cause or knows himself to be likely to cause is not grievous, but the hurt which he actually causes is grievous, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine which may extend to $7,500, or with both.

335A.section_title: Allowing neglect, physical or sexual abuse of domestic worker or vulnerable person

raw.marginal_note: Allowing neglect, physical or sexual abuse of domestic worker or vulnerable person
raw.text: —(1) A person ( A ) shall be guilty of an offence if — ( a ) a domestic worker ( B ) suffers neglect, hurt, grievous hurt, sexual abuse or any injury to B ’s mental health as a result of the unlawful act of another person ( C ); ( b ) A knew or had reason to believe that B was at risk of such neglect, hurt, grievous hurt, sexual abuse or injury caused by C ; ( c ) A was at the time of the risk mentioned in paragraph ( b ) the employer of B , a member of B ’s employer’s household or an employment agent of B ; ( d ) A failed to take such steps as A could reasonably have been expected to take in A ’s circumstances to protect B from the risk mentioned in paragraph ( b ); and ( e ) the act occurred in circumstances of the kind that A foresaw or ought to have foreseen. (2) A person ( A ) shall be guilty of an offence if — (3) For the purposes of this section, “unlawful act”, other than an act by A , is one that constitutes an offence that causes neglect, hurt, grievous hurt or sexual abuse to B or injury to B ’s mental health or would constitute such an offence but for being the act of — (4) A ’s circumstances mentioned in subsections (1)( d ) and (2)( d ), include but is not limited to A ’s past or present experiences of suffering neglect, hurt, grievous hurt, sexual abuse or any injury to A ’s mental health as a result of an unlawful act by C . (5) Any person who is guilty of an offence under subsection (1) or (2) shall on conviction — (6) In this section —

sub_items.text: —(1) A person ( A ) shall be guilty of an offence if — ( a ) a domestic worker ( B ) suffers neglect, hurt, grievous hurt, sexual abuse or any injury to B ’s mental health as a result of the unlawful act of another person ( C ); ( b ) A knew or had reason to believe that B was at risk of such neglect, hurt, grievous hurt, sexual abuse or injury caused by C ; ( c ) A was at the time of the risk mentioned in paragraph ( b ) the employer of B , a member of B ’s employer’s household or an employment agent of B ; ( d ) A failed to take such steps as A could reasonably have been expected to take in A ’s circumstances to protect B from the risk mentioned in paragraph ( b ); and ( e ) the act occurred in circumstances of the kind that A foresaw or ought to have foreseen.

sub_items.label: (a)

sub_items.label: (b)

sub_items.label: (c)

sub_items.label: (d)

sub_items.label: (e)

(2) A person ( A ) shall be guilty of an offence if — ( a ) a vulnerable person ( B ) suffers neglect, hurt, grievous hurt, sexual abuse or any injury to B ’s mental health as a result of the unlawful act of another person ( C ); ( b ) A knew or had reason to believe that B was at risk of neglect, hurt, grievous hurt, sexual abuse or injury caused by C ; ( c ) A had, at the time of the risk mentioned in paragraph ( b ), the custody, charge or care of B ; ( d ) A failed to take such steps as A could reasonably have been expected to take in A ’s circumstances to protect B from the risk mentioned in paragraph ( b ); and ( e ) the act occurred in circumstances of the kind that A foresaw or ought to have foreseen.

sub_items.label: (2)(a)
sub_items.text: a vulnerable person ( B ) suffers neglect, hurt, grievous hurt, sexual abuse or any injury to B ’s mental health as a result of the unlawful act of another person ( C );

sub_items.label: (2)(b)
sub_items.text: A knew or had reason to believe that B was at risk of neglect, hurt, grievous hurt, sexual abuse or injury caused by C ;

sub_items.label: (2)(c)
sub_items.text: A had, at the time of the risk mentioned in paragraph ( b ), the custody, charge or care of B ;

sub_items.label: (2)(d)
sub_items.text: A failed to take such steps as A could reasonably have been expected to take in A ’s circumstances to protect B from the risk mentioned in paragraph ( b ); and

sub_items.label: (2)(e)
sub_items.text: the act occurred in circumstances of the kind that A foresaw or ought to have foreseen.

(3) For the purposes of this section, “unlawful act”, other than an act by A , is one that constitutes an offence that causes neglect, hurt, grievous hurt or sexual abuse to B or injury to B ’s mental health or would constitute such an offence but for being the act of — ( a ) a person below the age specified in section 82; ( b ) a person of the age specified in section 83 who had not attained sufficient maturity of understanding to judge of the nature and consequence of the act; ( c ) a person entitled to rely on a defence of unsoundness of mind; ( d ) a person entitled to rely on a defence of intoxication; or ( e ) a person entitled to rely on a defence of mistake of fact.

sub_items.label: (3)(a)
sub_items.text: a person below the age specified in section 82;

sub_items.label: (3)(b)
sub_items.text: a person of the age specified in section 83 who had not attained sufficient maturity of understanding to judge of the nature and consequence of the act;

sub_items.label: (3)(c)

sub_items.label: (3)(e)

(4) A ’s circumstances mentioned in subsections (1)( d ) and (2)( d ), include but is not limited to A ’s past or present experiences of suffering neglect, hurt, grievous hurt, sexual abuse or any injury to A ’s mental health as a result of an unlawful act by C .

(5) Any person who is guilty of an offence under subsection (1) or (2) shall on conviction — ( a ) in the case where death is caused to the domestic worker or vulnerable person, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 7 years, or with fine which may extend to $20,000, or with both; and ( b ) in any other case, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 4 years, or with fine which may extend to $4,000, or with both.

sub_items.label: (5)(a)
sub_items.text: in the case where death is caused to the domestic worker or vulnerable person, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 7 years, or with fine which may extend to $20,000, or with both; and

sub_items.label: (5)(b)
sub_items.text: in any other case, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 4 years, or with fine which may extend to $4,000, or with both.

(6) In this section — “domestic worker”, “employer”, “employment agent” and “member of the employer’s household” have the meanings given by section 73(4); “neglect” has the meaning given by section 304B(2); “sexual abuse” means abuse caused to a victim by the commission of a sexual offence within the meaning of section 2(1) of the Criminal Procedure Code 2010; “vulnerable person” has the meaning given by section 74A(5).

SECTION 335B: Punishment for act which endangers life or personal safety of others with knowledge or belief that it is likely to cause death
============================================================
Jurisdiction: singapore
  act: Penal Code 1871
  source: https://sso.agc.gov.sg/Act/PC1871?ProvIds=pr335B-#pr335B-
Effective: 1872-01-01

Elements of the offence:
  Conjunctive requirement (all_of): every following limb must be proved:
    Physical element (actus reus): Does any act that endangers human life or the personal safety of others
    Mental element (mens rea): Knowing or believing that such act is likely to cause death

Penalty:
  Shall be punished with imprisonment for a term of not less than no time and not more than 7 years.

metadata.summary: Makes it an offence to do an act endangering life or personal safety while knowing or believing that the act is likely to cause death.

336.section_title: Punishment for act which endangers life or the personal safety of others

raw.marginal_note: Punishment for act which endangers life or the personal safety of others
raw.text: Whoever does any act so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life or the personal safety of others, shall be punished — ( a ) in the case of a rash act, with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 6 months, or with fine which may extend to $2,500, or with both; or ( b ) in the case of a negligent act, with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 3 months, or with fine which may extend to $1,500, or with both.

sub_items.label: (a)
sub_items.text: in the case of a rash act, with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 6 months, or with fine which may extend to $2,500, or with both; or

sub_items.label: (b)
sub_items.text: in the case of a negligent act, with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 3 months, or with fine which may extend to $1,500, or with both.

elements_detail.text: Doing the act so negligently

elements_detail.text: The act endangers the personal safety of others

Elements of the offence:

  Shall be punished with imprisonment for a term of not less than no time and not more than 3 months, or with a fine of not less than S$0.00 and not more than S$1,500.00, or with any combination thereof.

metadata.summary: Section 336 punishes a person who does a rash or negligent act that endangers human life or the personal safety of others.

337.section_title: Causing hurt by an act which endangers life or the personal safety of others

raw.marginal_note: Causing hurt by an act which endangers life or the personal safety of others
raw.text: Whoever causes hurt to any person by doing any act so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life or the personal safety of others, shall be punished — ( a ) in the case of a rash act, with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine which may extend to $5,000, or with both; or ( b ) in the case of a negligent act, with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 6 months, or with fine which may extend to $2,500, or with both.

sub_items.label: (a)
sub_items.text: in the case of a rash act, with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine which may extend to $5,000, or with both; or

sub_items.label: (b)
sub_items.text: in the case of a negligent act, with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 6 months, or with fine which may extend to $2,500, or with both.

338.section_title: Causing grievous hurt by an act which endangers life or the personal safety of others

raw.marginal_note: Causing grievous hurt by an act which endangers life or the personal safety of others
raw.text: Whoever causes grievous hurt to any person by doing any act so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life or the personal safety of others, shall be punished — ( a ) in the case of a rash act, with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 4 years, or with fine which may extend to $10,000, or with both; or ( b ) in the case of a negligent act, with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 2 years, or with fine which may extend to $5,000, or with both.

raw.marginal_note: Wrongful restraint
raw.text: Whoever voluntarily obstructs any person, so as to prevent that person from proceeding in any direction in which that person has a right to proceed, is said wrongfully to restrain that person.

sub_items.text: Exception. —The obstruction of a private way over land or water which a person in good faith believes himself to have a lawful right to obstruct, is not an offence within the meaning of this section.
Illustration
A obstructs a path along which Z has a right to pass, A not believing in good faith that he has a right to stop the path. Z is thereby prevented from passing. A wrongfully restrains Z .

elements_detail.label: obstruction
elements_detail.text: Whoever voluntarily obstructs any person

elements_detail.label: prevention
elements_detail.text: so as to prevent that person from proceeding in any direction

elements_detail.label: right_to_proceed
elements_detail.text: in which that person has a right to proceed

transpiled.english:
SECTION 339: Wrongful restraint
============================================================
Jurisdiction: singapore
  act: Penal Code 1871
  source: https://sso.agc.gov.sg/Act/PC1871?ProvIds=pr339-#pr339-
Effective: 1872-01-01

Definitions:
  "wrongful_restraint" means Whoever voluntarily obstructs any person, so as to prevent that person from proceeding in any direction in which that person has a right to proceed, is said wrongfully to restrain that person.
  "voluntarily_obstructs" means voluntarily obstructs any person
  "prevents_proceeding" means prevent that person from proceeding in any direction
  "right_to_proceed" means in which that person has a right to proceed

Elements of the offence:
  Conjunctive requirement (all_of): every following limb must be proved:
    Physical element (actus reus): Whoever voluntarily obstructs any person
    Physical element (actus reus): so as to prevent that person from proceeding in any direction
    Circumstance: in which that person has a right to proceed

Exceptions:
  [private_way_good_faith] If The obstruction of a private way over land or water which a person in good faith believes himself to have a lawful right to obstruct, is not an offence within the meaning of this section.

Illustrations:

Illustration illustration_a: A obstructs a path along which Z has a right to pass, A not believing in good faith that he has a right to stop the path. Z is thereby prevented from passing. A wrongfully restrains Z .
  Provenance: statutory illustration attached to Section 339.

metadata.summary: Defines wrongful restraint as voluntary obstruction that prevents a person from proceeding where that person has a right to proceed.

34.section_title: Each of several persons liable for an act done by all, in like manner as if done by him alone

raw.marginal_note: Each of several persons liable for an act done by all, in like manner as if done by him alone
raw.text: When a criminal act is done by several persons, in furtherance of the common intention of all, each of such persons is liable for that act in the same manner as if the act were done by him alone.

elements_detail.label: criminal_act_done
elements_detail.text: A criminal act is done

elements_detail.label: several_persons
elements_detail.text: The criminal act is done by several persons

elements_detail.label: common_intention_of_all
elements_detail.text: The criminal act is done in furtherance of the common intention of all

elements_detail.label: liable_as_alone
elements_detail.text: Each of such persons is liable for that act in the same manner as if the act were done by him alone

transpiled.english:
SECTION 34: Each of several persons liable for an act done by all, in like manner as if done by him alone
============================================================
Jurisdiction: singapore
  act: Penal Code 1871
  source: https://sso.agc.gov.sg/Act/PC1871?ProvIds=pr34-#pr34-
Effective: 1872-01-01

Definitions:
  "criminal_act" means A criminal act done by several persons
  "common_intention" means The common intention of all such persons
  "individual_liability" means Each of such persons is liable for that act in the same manner as if the act were done by him alone

Elements of the offence:
  Conjunctive requirement (all_of): every following limb must be proved:
    Physical element (actus reus): A criminal act is done
    Circumstance: The criminal act is done by several persons
    Mental element (mens rea): The criminal act is done in furtherance of the common intention of all
    Circumstance: Each of such persons is liable for that act in the same manner as if the act were done by him alone

metadata.summary: When a criminal act is done by several persons in furtherance of their common intention, each is liable as if acting alone.

raw.marginal_note: Wrongful confinement
raw.text: Whoever wrongfully restrains any person in such a manner as to prevent that person from proceeding beyond certain circumscribing limits, is said “wrongfully to confine” that person.

sub_items.text: Illustrations

sub_items.label: a
sub_items.text: ( a ) A causes Z to go within a walled space, and locks Z in. Z is thus prevented from proceeding in any direction beyond the circumscribing line of wall. A wrongfully confines Z .

sub_items.label: b
sub_items.text: ( b ) A places men with firearms at the outlets of a building and tells Z that they will fire at Z if Z attempts to leave the building. A wrongfully confines Z .

SECTION 340: Wrongful confinement
============================================================
Jurisdiction: singapore
  act: Penal Code 1871
  source: https://sso.agc.gov.sg/Act/PC1871?ProvIds=pr340-#pr340-
Effective: 1872-01-01

Definitions:
  "wrongful_confinement" means Whoever wrongfully restrains any person in such a manner as to prevent that person from proceeding beyond certain circumscribing limits, is said “wrongfully to confine” that person.
  "wrongful_restraint" means wrongfully restrains any person
  "preventing_proceeding_beyond_limits" means prevent that person from proceeding beyond certain circumscribing limits

metadata.summary: Defines wrongful confinement as wrongful restraint that prevents a person from proceeding beyond circumscribing limits.

raw.marginal_note: Punishment for wrongful restraint
raw.text: Whoever wrongfully restrains any person shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to $1,500, or with both.

raw.marginal_note: Punishment for wrongful confinement
raw.text: Whoever wrongfully confines any person shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 3 years, or with fine, or with both.

metadata.summary: Sets the punishment for committing wrongful confinement.

345.section_title: Wrongful confinement of person for whose liberation a writ has been issued

raw.marginal_note: Wrongful confinement of person for whose liberation a writ has been issued
raw.text: Whoever keeps any person in wrongful confinement, knowing that a writ for the liberation of that person has been duly issued, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 2 years, in addition to any term of imprisonment to which he may be liable under any other section of this Code.

raw.marginal_note: Wrongful confinement in secret
raw.text: Whoever wrongfully confines any person in such a manner as to indicate an intention that the confinement of that person may not be known to any person interested in the person so confined, or to any public servant, or that the place of such confinement may not be known to or discovered by any such person or public servant as hereinbefore mentioned, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 2 years, in addition to any other punishment to which he may be liable for such wrongful confinement.

============================================================
Jurisdiction: singapore
  act: Penal Code 1871
  source: https://sso.agc.gov.sg/Act/PC1871?ProvIds=pr346-#pr346-

347.section_title: Wrongful confinement for the purpose of extorting property or constraining to an illegal act

raw.marginal_note: Wrongful confinement for the purpose of extorting property or constraining to an illegal act
raw.text: Whoever wrongfully confines any person for the purpose of extorting from the person confined, or from any person interested in the person confined, any property or valuable security, or of constraining the person confined, or any person interested in such person, to do anything illegal or to give any information which may facilitate the commission of an offence, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 3 years, and shall also be liable to fine.

elements_detail.label: constraining_illegal_act
elements_detail.text: For the purpose of constraining the person confined, or any person interested in such person, to do anything illegal

elements_detail.label: constraining_offence_facilitating_information
elements_detail.text: For the purpose of constraining the person confined, or any person interested in such person, to give any information which may facilitate the commission of an offence

SECTION 347: Wrongful confinement for the purpose of extorting property or constraining to an illegal act
============================================================
Jurisdiction: singapore
  act: Penal Code 1871
  source: https://sso.agc.gov.sg/Act/PC1871?ProvIds=pr347-#pr347-
Effective: 1872-01-01

Definitions:
  "wrongful_confinement" means wrongfully confines any person
  "person_interested" means any person interested in the person confined
  "valuable_security" means valuable security
  "illegal_act" means anything illegal
  "offence_facilitating_information" means any information which may facilitate the commission of an offence

Elements of the offence:
  Conjunctive requirement (all_of): every following limb must be proved:
    Physical element (actus reus): Whoever wrongfully confines any person
    Alternative requirement (any_of): proving one following limb is enough:
      Mental element (mens rea): For the purpose of extorting from the person confined, or from any person interested in the person confined, any property or valuable security
      Mental element (mens rea): For the purpose of constraining the person confined, or any person interested in such person, to do anything illegal
      Mental element (mens rea): For the purpose of constraining the person confined, or any person interested in such person, to give any information which may facilitate the commission of an offence

  Shall be punished with imprisonment for a term of not less than no time and not more than 3 years.

metadata.summary: Punishes wrongful confinement used to extort property or valuable security, or to coerce an illegal act or offence-facilitating information.

348.section_title: Wrongful confinement for the purpose of extorting confession or of compelling restoration of property

raw.marginal_note: Wrongful confinement for the purpose of extorting confession or of compelling restoration of property
raw.text: Whoever wrongfully confines any person for the purpose of extorting from the person confined, or from any person interested in the person confined, any confession or any information which may lead to the detection of an offence or misconduct, or for the purpose of constraining the person confined, or any person interested in the person confined, to restore, or to cause the restoration of any property or valuable security, or to satisfy any claim or demand, or to give information which may lead to the restoration of any property or valuable security, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 3 years, and shall also be liable to fine.

elements_detail.label: wrongful_confinement

raw.marginal_note: Force
raw.text: A person is said to use force to another if he causes motion, change of motion, or cessation of motion to that other, or if he causes to any substance such motion, or change of motion, or cessation of motion as brings that substance into contact with any part of that other’s body, or with anything which that other is wearing or carrying, or with anything so situated that such contact affects that other’s sense of feeling:

35.section_title: When such an act is criminal by reason of its being done with a criminal knowledge or intention

raw.marginal_note: When such an act is criminal by reason of its being done with a criminal knowledge or intention
raw.text: Whenever an act, which is criminal only by reason of its being done with a criminal knowledge or intention, is done by several persons, each of such persons who joins in the act with such knowledge or intention, is liable for the act in the same manner as if the act were done by him alone with that knowledge or intention.

elements_detail.label: criminal_only_by_knowledge_or_intention
elements_detail.text: The act is criminal only by reason of its being done with a criminal knowledge or intention

elements_detail.label: done_by_several_persons
elements_detail.text: The act is done by several persons

elements_detail.label: joins_in_act
elements_detail.text: The person joins in the act

elements_detail.label: with_such_knowledge_or_intention
elements_detail.text: The person joins in the act with such knowledge or intention

elements_detail.label: liable_as_if_done_alone
elements_detail.text: The person is liable for the act in the same manner as if the act were done by him alone with that knowledge or intention

transpiled.english:
SECTION 35: When such an act is criminal by reason of its being done with a criminal knowledge or intention
============================================================
Jurisdiction: singapore
  act: Penal Code 1871
  source: https://sso.agc.gov.sg/Act/PC1871?ProvIds=pr35-#pr35-
Effective: 1872-01-01

Definitions:
  "knowledge_or_intention_dependent_act" means An act, which is criminal only by reason of its being done with a criminal knowledge or intention
  "several_persons" means Several persons by whom the act is done
  "joining_person" means Each of such persons who joins in the act with such knowledge or intention
  "same_manner_liability" means Liable for the act in the same manner as if the act were done by him alone with that knowledge or intention

Elements of the offence:
  Conjunctive requirement (all_of): every following limb must be proved:
    Circumstance: The act is criminal only by reason of its being done with a criminal knowledge or intention
    Physical element (actus reus): The act is done by several persons
    Physical element (actus reus): The person joins in the act
    Mental element (mens rea): The person joins in the act with such knowledge or intention
    Circumstance: The person is liable for the act in the same manner as if the act were done by him alone with that knowledge or intention

metadata.summary: Section 35 makes each person who joins in a knowledge- or intention-dependent criminal act liable as if acting alone with that knowledge or intention.

raw.marginal_note: Criminal force
raw.text: Whoever intentionally uses force to any person, without that person’s consent, in order to cause the committing of any offence, or intending by the use of such force illegally to cause, or knowing it to be likely that by the use of such force he will illegally cause injury, fear or annoyance to the person to whom the force is used, is said to use criminal force to that other.

sub_items.text: Illustrations

sub_items.label: a
sub_items.text: ( a ) Z is sitting in a moored boat on a river. A unfastens the moorings, and thus intentionally causes the boat to drift down the stream. Here A intentionally causes motion to Z , and he does this by disposing substances in such a manner that the motion is produced without any other act on any person’s part. A has therefore intentionally used force to Z ; and if he has done so without Z ’s consent, in order to cause the committing of any offence, or intending or knowing it to be likely that this use of force will cause injury, fear or annoyance to Z , A has used criminal force to Z .

sub_items.label: b
sub_items.text: ( b ) Z is riding a horse. A lashes Z ’s horse, and thereby causes it to quicken its pace. Here A has caused change of motion to Z by inducing the horse to change its motion. A has therefore used force to Z ; and if A has done this without Z ’s consent, intending or knowing it to be likely that he may thereby injure, frighten or annoy Z , A has used criminal force to Z .

sub_items.label: c
sub_items.text: ( c ) Z is riding a horse. A , intending to cause hurt to Z , seizes the horse and stops it. Here A has caused cessation of motion to Z , and he has done this by his own bodily power. A has therefore used force to Z ; and as A has acted thus intentionally without Z ’s consent, in order to cause the commission of an offence, A has used criminal force to Z .

sub_items.label: d
sub_items.text: ( d ) A intentionally pushes against Z in the street. Here A has by his own bodily power moved his own person so as to bring it into contact with Z . He has therefore intentionally used force to Z , and if he has done so without Z ’s consent, intending or knowing it to be likely that he may thereby injure, frighten or annoy Z , he has used criminal force to Z .

sub_items.label: e
sub_items.text: ( e ) A throws a stone, intending or knowing it to be likely that the stone will be thus brought into contact with Z , or with Z ’s clothes, or with something carried by Z , or that it will strike water and dash up the water against Z ’s clothes, or something carried by Z . Here if the throwing of the stone produces the effect of causing any substance to come into contact with Z , or Z ’s clothes, A has used force to Z ; and if he has done so without Z ’s consent, intending thereby to injure, frighten or annoy Z , he has used criminal force to Z .

sub_items.label: f
sub_items.text: ( f ) A intentionally pulls up a woman’s veil. Here A intentionally uses force to her; and if he does so without her consent, intending or knowing it to be likely that he may thereby injure, frighten or annoy her, he has used criminal force to her.

sub_items.label: g
sub_items.text: ( g ) Z is bathing. A pours into the bath water which he knows to be boiling. Here A intentionally by his own bodily power causes such motion in the boiling water as brings that water into contact with Z , or with other water so situated that such contact must affect Z ’s sense of feeling; A has therefore intentionally used force to Z ; and if he has done this without Z ’s consent, intending or knowing it to be likely that he may thereby cause injury, fear or annoyance to Z , A has used criminal force to Z .

sub_items.label: h
sub_items.text: ( h ) A incites a dog to spring upon Z , without Z ’s consent. Here, if A intends to cause injury, fear or annoyance to Z , he uses criminal force to Z .

elements_detail.label: intentional_use_of_force
elements_detail.text: Whoever intentionally uses force to any person

elements_detail.label: absence_of_consent
elements_detail.text: without that person’s consent

elements_detail.label: purpose_to_cause_offence
elements_detail.text: in order to cause the committing of any offence

elements_detail.label: intent_to_cause_illegally
elements_detail.text: intending by the use of such force illegally to cause injury, fear or annoyance to the person to whom the force is used

elements_detail.label: knowledge_likely_to_cause_illegally
elements_detail.text: knowing it to be likely that by the use of such force he will illegally cause injury, fear or annoyance to the person to whom the force is used

raw.marginal_note: Assault
raw.text: Whoever makes any gesture or any preparation, intending or knowing it to be likely that such gesture or preparation will cause any person present to apprehend that he who makes that gesture or preparation is about to use criminal force to that person, is said to commit an assault.

sub_items.text: Explanation. — Mere words do not amount to an assault. But the words which a person uses may give to his gestures or preparations such a meaning as may make those gestures or preparations amount to an assault.
Illustrations

sub_items.label: a
sub_items.text: ( a ) A shakes his fist at Z , intending or knowing it to be likely that he may thereby cause Z to believe that A is about to strike Z . A has committed an assault.

sub_items.label: b
sub_items.text: ( b ) A begins to unloose the muzzle of a ferocious dog, intending or knowing it to be likely that he may thereby cause Z to believe that he is about to cause the dog to attack Z. A has committed an assault upon Z .

sub_items.label: c
sub_items.text: ( c ) A takes up a stick, saying to Z , “I will give you a beating”. Here, though the words used by A could in no case amount to an assault, and though the mere gesture, unaccompanied by any other circumstances might not amount to an assault, the gesture explained by the words may amount to an assault.

elements_detail.label: gesture_or_preparation
elements_detail.text: Whoever makes any gesture or any preparation

elements_detail.label: intention
elements_detail.text: intending that such gesture or preparation will cause any person present to apprehend that he who makes that gesture or preparation is about to use criminal force to that person

elements_detail.label: knowledge
elements_detail.text: knowing it to be likely that such gesture or preparation will cause any person present to apprehend that he who makes that gesture or preparation is about to use criminal force to that person

elements_detail.label: apprehension_of_imminent_criminal_force
elements_detail.text: such gesture or preparation will cause any person present to apprehend that he who makes that gesture or preparation is about to use criminal force to that person

352.section_title: Punishment for using criminal force otherwise than on grave and sudden provocation

raw.marginal_note: Punishment for using criminal force otherwise than on grave and sudden provocation
raw.text: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person otherwise than on grave and sudden provocation given by that person, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 3 months, or with fine which may extend to $1,500, or with both.

sub_items.text: Explanation. —Grave and sudden provocation will not mitigate the punishment for an offence under this section, if the provocation is sought or voluntarily provoked by the offender as an excuse for the offence; or

if the offender knows or has reason to believe that the provocation is given by anything done in obedience to the law or by a public servant in the lawful exercise of the powers of such public servant; or
if the provocation is given by anything done in the lawful exercise of the right of private defence.
Whether the provocation was grave and sudden enough to mitigate the offence, is a question of fact.

SECTION 352: Punishment for using criminal force otherwise than on grave and sudden provocation
============================================================
Jurisdiction: singapore
  act: Penal Code 1871
  source: https://sso.agc.gov.sg/Act/PC1871?ProvIds=pr352-#pr352-
Effective: 1872-01-01

Definitions:
  "explanation" means Explanation. —Grave and sudden provocation will not mitigate the punishment for an offence under this section, if the provocation is sought or voluntarily provoked by the offender as an excuse for the offence; or
  "proviso_law_or_public_servant" means if the offender knows or has reason to believe that the provocation is given by anything done in obedience to the law or by a public servant in the lawful exercise of the powers of such public servant; or
  "amendment_marker" means [Act 23 of 2021 wef 01/03/2022]
  "proviso_private_defence" means if the provocation is given by anything done in the lawful exercise of the right of private defence.
  "question_of_fact" means Whether the provocation was grave and sudden enough to mitigate the offence, is a question of fact.

Penalty:
  Shall be punished with imprisonment for a term of not less than no time and not more than 3 months, or with a fine of not less than S$0.00 and not more than S$1,500.00, or with any combination thereof.

  Additionally: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person otherwise than on grave and sudden provocation given by that person, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 3 months, or with fine which may extend to $1,500, or with both.

353.section_title: Using criminal force to deter a public servant from discharge of his duty

raw.marginal_note: Using criminal force to deter a public servant from discharge of his duty
raw.text: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person being a public servant in the execution of his duty as such public servant, or with intent to prevent or deter that person from discharging his duty as such public servant, or in consequence of anything done or attempted to be done by such person in the lawful discharge of his duty as such public servant, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 4 years, or with fine, or with both.

elements_detail.label: consequence_of_lawful_discharge
elements_detail.text: The assault or criminal force is in consequence of anything done or attempted to be done by such person in the lawful discharge of his duty as such public servant

354.section_title: Assault or Criminal Force to Outrage Modesty

raw.marginal_note: Assault or use of criminal force to a person with intent to outrage modesty
raw.text: —(1) Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person, intending to outrage or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby outrage the modesty of that person, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 3 years, or with fine, or with caning, or with any combination of such punishments. (2) Whoever commits an offence under subsection (1) against any person below 14 years of age shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 5 years, or with fine, or with caning, or with any combination of such punishments.

sub_items.text: —(1) Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person, intending to outrage or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby outrage the modesty of that person, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 3 years, or with fine, or with caning, or with any combination of such punishments.

(2) Whoever commits an offence under subsection (1) against any person below 14 years of age shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 5 years, or with fine, or with caning, or with any combination of such punishments.

elements_detail.text: Intent to outrage modesty or knowledge that act is likely to outrage modesty

elements_detail.label: force_or_assault
elements_detail.text: Assault or use of criminal force on any person

elements_detail.text: Act outrages the modesty of the person

SECTION 354: Assault or Criminal Force to Outrage Modesty
============================================================
Effective: 1872-01-01

Definitions:
  "outrage_modesty" means Using criminal force or assault on any person with intent to or knowing it likely to outrage the modesty of that person
  "criminal_force" means Intentionally using force on any person without that person's consent
  "assault" means Making any gesture or preparation intending or knowing it to be likely to cause apprehension of criminal force

Elements of the offence:
  Mental element (mens rea): Intent to outrage modesty or knowledge that act is likely to outrage modesty
  Physical element (actus reus): Assault or use of criminal force on any person
  Circumstance: Act outrages the modesty of the person

Penalty:
  Shall be punished with imprisonment for a term of not less than no time and not more than 5 years, or with a fine of not less than S$0.00 and not more than S$10,000.00, or with caning with not less than 0 and not more than 6 strokes, or with any combination thereof.

Illustrations:

Illustration example1: A grabs B's arm and touches B inappropriately without consent. A intends to outrage B's modesty. A is guilty under section 354.
  Provenance: statutory illustration attached to Section 354.
Illustration example2: A, in a crowded place, deliberately presses against B knowing it is likely to outrage B's modesty. A is guilty under section 354.
  Provenance: statutory illustration attached to Section 354.

36.section_title: Effect caused partly by act and partly by omission

raw.marginal_note: Effect caused partly by act and partly by omission
raw.text: Wherever the causing of a certain effect, or an attempt to cause that effect, by an act or by an omission, is an offence, it is to be understood that the causing of that effect partly by an act and partly by an omission is the same offence.

sub_items.text: Illustration
A intentionally causes Z ’s death, partly by illegally omitting to give Z food, and partly by beating Z . A has committed murder.

  "partly_act_partly_omission" means The causing of that effect partly by an act and partly by an omission
  "same_offence" means The causing of that effect partly by an act and partly by an omission is the same offence

raw.marginal_note: Kidnapping from Singapore
raw.text: Whoever conveys any person beyond the limits of Singapore without the consent of that person, or of some person legally authorised to consent on behalf of that person, is said to kidnap that person from Singapore.

raw.marginal_note: Kidnapping from lawful guardianship
raw.text: Whoever takes or entices any minor below 16 years of age, or any person of unsound mind, out of the keeping of the lawful guardian of such minor or person of unsound mind, without the consent of such guardian, is said to kidnap such minor or person from lawful guardianship.

sub_items.text: Explanation. —The words “lawful guardian” in this section include any person lawfully entrusted with the care or custody of such minor or other person.

Exception. —This section does not extend to the act of any person who in good faith believes himself to be the father of an illegitimate child or who in good faith believes himself to be entitled to the lawful custody of such child, unless such act is committed for an immoral or unlawful purpose.

  "kidnapping_from_lawful_guardianship" means Taking or enticing any minor under 14, or any person of unsound mind, out of the keeping of the lawful guardian without consent

364.section_title: Kidnapping or abducting in order to murder

raw.marginal_note: Kidnapping or abducting in order to murder
raw.text: Whoever kidnaps or abducts any person in order that such person may be murdered, or may be so disposed of as to be put in danger of being murdered, shall be punished with death or imprisonment for life and shall, if he is not sentenced to death, also be liable to caning.

sub_items.text: Illustrations

sub_items.label: a
sub_items.text: ( a ) A kidnaps Z from Singapore, intending or knowing it to be likely that Z may be sacrificed to an idol. A has committed the offence defined in this section.

sub_items.label: b
sub_items.text: ( b ) A forcibly carries or entices B away from his home in order that B may be murdered. A has committed the offence defined in this section.

368.section_title: Wrongfully concealing or keeping in confinement a kidnapped person

37.section_title: Cooperation by doing one of several acts constituting an offence

raw.marginal_note: Cooperation by doing one of several acts constituting an offence
raw.text: When an offence is committed by means of several acts, whoever intentionally cooperates in the commission of that offence by doing any one of those acts, either singly or jointly with any other person, commits that offence.

sub_items.text: Illustrations

sub_items.label: a
sub_items.text: ( a ) A and B agree to murder Z , by severally, and at different times, giving him small doses of poison. A and B administer the poison, according to the agreement, with intent to murder Z . Z dies from the effects of the several doses of poison so administered to him. Here A and B intentionally cooperate in the commission of murder, and as each of them does an act by which the death is caused, they are both guilty of the offence, though their acts are separate.

sub_items.label: b
sub_items.text: ( b ) A and B are joint jailors, and as such have the charge of Z , a prisoner, alternately for 6 hours at a time. A and B , intending to cause Z ’s death, knowingly cooperate in causing that effect by illegally omitting, each during the time of his attendance, to furnish Z with food supplied to them for that purpose. Z dies of hunger. Both A and B are guilty of the murder of Z .

sub_items.label: c
sub_items.text: ( c ) A , a jailor, has the charge of Z , a prisoner. A , intending to cause Z ’s death, illegally omits to supply Z with food; in consequence of which Z is much reduced in strength, but the starvation is not sufficient to cause his death. A is dismissed from his office, and B succeeds him. B , without collusion or cooperation with A , illegally omits to supply Z with food, knowing that he is likely thereby to cause Z ’s death. Z dies of hunger. B is guilty of murder; but as A did not cooperate with B , A is guilty only of an attempt to commit murder.

elements_detail.label: offence_committed_by_several_acts
elements_detail.text: An offence is committed by means of several acts

elements_detail.label: intentional_cooperation
elements_detail.text: The person intentionally cooperates in the commission of that offence

elements_detail.label: doing_one_act
elements_detail.text: The person does any one of those acts

elements_detail.label: singly_or_jointly
elements_detail.text: The act is done either singly or jointly with any other person

elements_detail.label: commits_that_offence
elements_detail.text: The person commits that offence

  Provenance: statutory illustration attached to Section 37.
Illustration illustration_b: ( b ) A and B are joint jailors, and as such have the charge of Z , a prisoner, alternately for 6 hours at a time. A and B , intending to cause Z ’s death, knowingly cooperate in causing that effect by illegally omitting, each during the time of his attendance, to furnish Z with food supplied to them for that purpose. Z dies of hunger. Both A and B are guilty of the murder of Z .
  Provenance: statutory illustration attached to Section 37.
Illustration illustration_c: ( c ) A , a jailor, has the charge of Z , a prisoner. A , intending to cause Z ’s death, illegally omits to supply Z with food; in consequence of which Z is much reduced in strength, but the starvation is not sufficient to cause his death. A is dismissed from his office, and B succeeds him. B , without collusion or cooperation with A , illegally omits to supply Z with food, knowing that he is likely thereby to cause Z ’s death. Z dies of hunger. B is guilty of murder; but as A did not cooperate with B , A is guilty only of an attempt to commit murder.
  Provenance: statutory illustration attached to Section 37.

metadata.summary: A person who intentionally cooperates in a several-act offence by doing one of those acts commits that offence.

370.section_title: Buying or disposing of any person as a slave

raw.marginal_note: Buying or disposing of any person as a slave
raw.text: Whoever imports, exports, removes, buys, sells or disposes of any person as a slave, or accepts, receives or detains against his will any person as a slave, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 7 years, and shall also be liable to fine.

372.section_title: Selling minor for purposes of prostitution, etc.

raw.marginal_note: Selling minor for purposes of prostitution, etc.
raw.text: Whoever sells, lets to hire, or otherwise disposes of any person below 21 years of age with intent that such person shall at any age be employed or used for the purpose of prostitution or illicit intercourse with any person or for any unlawful and immoral purpose, or knowing it to be likely that such person will at any age be employed or used for any such purpose, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 10 years, and shall also be liable to fine.

sub_items.text: Explanation. —When a female below 21 years of age is sold, let for hire, or otherwise disposed of to a prostitute or to any person who keeps or manages a brothel, the person so disposing of such female shall, until the contrary is proved, be presumed to have disposed of her with the intent that she shall be used for the purpose of prostitution.

elements_detail.label: disposal_of_person_below_21
elements_detail.text: Whoever sells, lets to hire, or otherwise disposes of any person below 21 years of age

elements_detail.label: intent_person_employed_or_used_for_prohibited_purpose
elements_detail.text: With intent that such person shall at any age be employed or used for the purpose of prostitution or illicit intercourse with any person or for any unlawful and immoral purpose

elements_detail.label: knowledge_likely_person_employed_or_used_for_prohibited_purpose
elements_detail.text: Knowing it to be likely that such person will at any age be employed or used for any such purpose

Penalty:

metadata.summary: Selling, hiring out, or otherwise disposing of a person below 21 for prostitution, illicit intercourse, or another unlawful and immoral purpose is an offence.

sub_items.text: Explanation. —Any prostitute, or any person keeping or managing a brothel, who buys, hires or otherwise obtains possession of a female below 21 years of age shall, until the contrary is proved, be presumed to have obtained possession of such female with the intent that she shall be used for the purpose of prostitution.

373A.section_title: Importing woman for purposes of prostitution, etc.

raw.marginal_note: Importing woman for purposes of prostitution, etc.
raw.text: Whoever — ( a ) by any false pretence, false representation, or fraudulent or deceitful means, brings, or assists in bringing, into Singapore any woman with intent that such woman may be employed or used for the purpose of prostitution; ( b ) brings, or assists in bringing, into Singapore any woman with intent that such woman may be sold or bought for the purpose of prostitution; or ( c ) sells or buys any woman for the purpose of prostitution, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years, and shall also be liable to fine.

376A.section_title: Sexual penetration of minor below 16 years of age

raw.marginal_note: Sexual penetration of minor below 16 years of age
raw.text: —(1) Any person ( A ) who — ( a ) penetrates, with A ’s penis, the vagina, anus or mouth, as the case may be, of a person below 16 years of age ( B ); ( b ) sexually penetrates, with a part of A ’s body (other than A ’s penis, if a man) or anything else, the vagina or anus, as the case may be, of a person below 16 years of age ( B ); ( c ) causes a man below 16 years of age ( B ) to penetrate, with B ’s penis, the vagina, anus or mouth, as the case may be, of another person including A ; or ( d ) causes a person below 16 years of age ( B ) to sexually penetrate, with a part of B ’s body (other than B ’s penis, if a man) or anything else, the vagina or anus, as the case may be, of any person including A or B , shall be guilty of an offence. (1A) This section does not apply to an act of penetration mentioned in subsection (1) which would constitute an offence under section 375(1)( a ), 375(1)( b ) read with section 375(3), 375(1A)( a ), 375(1A)( b ) read with section 375(3), 376(2) (if the victim B is of or above 14 years of age) or 376(2) (if the victim B is below 14 years of age) read with section 376(4). (1B) To avoid doubt — (2) Whoever commits an offence under this section against a person ( B ) who is of or above 14 years of age but below 16 years of age — (3) Whoever commits an offence under this section against a person ( B ) who is below 14 years of age shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 20 years, and shall also be liable to fine or to caning. (4) No person shall be guilty of an offence under this section for an act of penetration against his or her spouse with the consent of that spouse.

raw.text: —(1) Any person ( A ) who is in a relationship that is exploitative of a person of or above 16 years of age but below 18 years of age ( B ) shall be guilty of an offence if A — ( a ) penetrates, with A ’s penis, if A is a man, the vagina, anus or mouth, as the case may be, of B ; ( b ) sexually penetrates, with a part of A ’s body (other than A ’s penis, if A is a man) or anything else, the vagina or anus, as the case may be, of B ; ( c ) causes B , if a man, to penetrate, with B ’s penis, the vagina, anus or mouth, as the case may be, of another person including A ; or ( d ) causes B to sexually penetrate, with a part of B ’s body (other than B ’s penis, if B is a man) or anything else, the vagina or anus, as the case may be, of any person including A or B . 376B.section_title: Commercial sex with minor below 18 years of age

raw.marginal_note: Commercial sex with minor below 18 years of age
raw.text: —(1) Any person who obtains for consideration the sexual services of a person, who is below 18 years of age, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 7 years, or with fine, or with both. (2) Any person who communicates with another person for the purpose of obtaining for consideration, the sexual services of a person who is below 18 years of age, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 2 years, or with fine, or with both. (3) No person shall be guilty of an offence under this section for any sexual services obtained from that person’s spouse. (4) In this section, “sexual services” means any sexual services involving —

sub_items.text: —(1) Any person who obtains for consideration the sexual services of a person, who is below 18 years of age, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 7 years, or with fine, or with both.

(2) Any person who communicates with another person for the purpose of obtaining for consideration, the sexual services of a person who is below 18 years of age, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 2 years, or with fine, or with both.
(3) No person shall be guilty of an offence under this section for any sexual services obtained from that person’s spouse.

(4) In this section, “sexual services” means any sexual services involving — ( a ) sexual penetration of the vagina or anus, as the case may be, of a person by a part of another person’s body (other than the penis) or by anything else; ( b ) penetration of the vagina, anus or mouth, as the case may be, of a person by a man’s penis; or ( c ) touching which is sexual of another person or of himself or herself.

metadata.summary: Creates an offence for an adult who, after prior contact, intentionally meets or arranges a meeting with a person below 16 in circumstances further specified by the section.

376EA.section_title: Exploitative sexual grooming of minor of or above 16 but below 18 years of age

raw.marginal_note: Exploitative sexual grooming of minor of or above 16 but below 18 years of age
raw.text: —(1) Any person of or above 18 years of age ( A ) shall be guilty of an offence if having met or communicated with another person ( B ) on at least one previous occasion — ( a ) A intentionally meets B or travels with the intention of meeting B or B travels to attend a meeting with A which A has either initiated or agreed to whether expressly or by implication; and ( b ) at the time of the acts mentioned in paragraph ( a ) — (2) In subsection (1), “relevant offence” means an offence under — (3) For the purposes of this section, it is immaterial whether the previous occasion of A having met or communicated with B mentioned in subsection (1) took place in or outside Singapore. (4) A person who is guilty of an offence under this section shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 3 years, or with fine, or with both.

sub_items.text: —(1) Any person of or above 18 years of age ( A ) shall be guilty of an offence if having met or communicated with another person ( B ) on at least one previous occasion — ( a ) A intentionally meets B or travels with the intention of meeting B or B travels to attend a meeting with A which A has either initiated or agreed to whether expressly or by implication; and ( b ) at the time of the acts mentioned in paragraph ( a ) —

sub_items.label: (a)

(2) In subsection (1), “relevant offence” means an offence under — ( a ) section 354, 354A, 355, 372, 373, 373A, 375, 376, 376AA, 376B, 376C, 376EC, 376EE, 376F, 376G, 376H, 377(1)( e ), ( f ), ( g ) or ( h ), 377B(3), 377BA, 377BB, 377BF or 377BL; ( b ) section 140(1) of the Women’s Charter 1961; or ( c ) section 7 of the Children and Young Persons Act 1 .

metadata.summary: Section 376EA criminalises exploitative sexual grooming by an adult against a minor aged 16 or 17 and provides for imprisonment up to 3 years, a fine, or both.

376EB.section_title: Sexual communication with minor below 16 years of age

raw.marginal_note: Sexual communication with minor below 16 years of age
raw.text: —(1) Any person of or above 18 years of age ( A ) shall be guilty of an offence if — ( a ) for the purpose of obtaining sexual gratification or of causing another person ( B ) humiliation, alarm or distress, A intentionally communicated with B ; ( b ) the communication is sexual; ( c ) at the time of the communication, B is below 16 years of age; and ( d ) A does not reasonably believe that B is of or above 16 years of age. (2) For the purposes of this section, it is immaterial — (3) A person who is guilty of an offence under this section shall — (4) No person shall be guilty of an offence under this section for any communication with his or her spouse with the consent of that spouse.

sub_items.text: —(1) Any person of or above 18 years of age ( A ) shall be guilty of an offence if — ( a ) for the purpose of obtaining sexual gratification or of causing another person ( B ) humiliation, alarm or distress, A intentionally communicated with B ; ( b ) the communication is sexual; ( c ) at the time of the communication, B is below 16 years of age; and ( d ) A does not reasonably believe that B is of or above 16 years of age.

sub_items.label: (a)
sub_items.text: for the purpose of obtaining sexual gratification or of causing another person ( B ) humiliation, alarm or distress, A intentionally communicated with B ;

sub_items.label: (b)
sub_items.text: the communication is sexual;

sub_items.label: (c)
sub_items.text: at the time of the communication, B is below 16 years of age; and

sub_items.label: (d)
sub_items.text: A does not reasonably believe that B is of or above 16 years of age.

(2) For the purposes of this section, it is immaterial — ( a ) whether B replied or responded to A ’s communication mentioned in subsection (1); and ( b ) whether such communication originated in Singapore provided that either A or B was in Singapore at the time of such communication.

sub_items.label: (2)(a)
sub_items.text: whether B replied or responded to A ’s communication mentioned in subsection (1); and

sub_items.label: (2)(b)
sub_items.text: whether such communication originated in Singapore provided that either A or B was in Singapore at the time of such communication.

(3) A person who is guilty of an offence under this section shall — ( a ) in the case where the offence is committed against a victim who is below 14 years of age and A does not reasonably believe that B is of or above that age, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 3 years, or with fine, or with both; or ( b ) in any other case, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 2 years, or with fine, or with both.

376F.section_title: Procurement of sexual activity with person with mental disability

raw.marginal_note: Procurement of sexual activity with person with mental disability
raw.text: —(1) Any person ( A ) shall be guilty of an offence if — ( a ) A intentionally touches another person ( B ) or intentionally incites B to touch A or B or another person; ( b ) the touching is sexual and B consents to the touching; ( c ) A obtains B ’s consent — ( ca ) B has a mental disability; and ( d ) A knows or could reasonably be expected to know that B has a mental disability. (2) Subject to subsection (3), a person who is guilty of an offence under this section shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 5 years, or with fine, or with caning, or with any combination of such punishments. (3) If the touching involved — (4) [ Deleted by Act 15 of 2019 ] (5) For the purposes of this section, “mental disability” means an impairment of or a disturbance in the functioning of the mind or brain resulting from any disability or disorder of the mind or brain which impairs the ability to make a proper judgement in the giving of consent to sexual touching.

sub_items.text: —(1) Any person ( A ) shall be guilty of an offence if — ( a ) A intentionally touches another person ( B ) or intentionally incites B to touch A or B or another person; ( b ) the touching is sexual and B consents to the touching; ( c ) A obtains B ’s consent — ( ca ) B has a mental disability; and ( d ) A knows or could reasonably be expected to know that B has a mental disability.

sub_items.label: (c)
sub_items.text: A obtains B ’s consent —

sub_items.label: (c)(i)
sub_items.text: where B is not A ’s spouse, by means of an inducement offered or given, a threat made or a deception practised by A for that purpose; or

sub_items.label: (c)(ii)
sub_items.text: where B is A ’s spouse, by means of a threat made or a deception practised by A for that purpose;

sub_items.text: B has a mental disability; and

sub_items.label: (d)
sub_items.text: A knows or could reasonably be expected to know that B has a mental disability.

(2) Subject to subsection (3), a person who is guilty of an offence under this section shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 5 years, or with fine, or with caning, or with any combination of such punishments.

(3) If the touching involved — ( a ) penetration of the vagina or anus, as the case may be, with a part of the body or anything else; or ( b ) penetration of the mouth with the penis, a person who is guilty of an offence under this section shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 20 years, and shall also be liable to a fine or to caning.

sub_items.label: (3)(a)

sub_items.label: (3)(b)

(4) [ Deleted by Act 15 of 2019 ]

(5) For the purposes of this section, “mental disability” means an impairment of or a disturbance in the functioning of the mind or brain resulting from any disability or disorder of the mind or brain which impairs the ability to make a proper judgement in the giving of consent to sexual touching.

raw.marginal_note: Incest
raw.text: —(1) Any person ( A ) of or above 16 years of age who — ( a ) penetrates, with A ’s penis, the vagina, anus or mouth, as the case may be, of a person of or above 16 years of age who is a close family relative ( B ); ( b ) sexually penetrates, with a part of A ’s body (other than A ’s penis, if A is a man) or anything else, the vagina or anus, as the case may be, of a person of or above 16 years of age who is a close family relative ( B ); ( c ) causes or permits a man of or above 16 years of age who is a close family relative ( B ) to penetrate, with B ’s penis, the vagina, anus or mouth, as the case may be, of A ; or ( d ) causes or permits a person of or above 16 years of age who is a close family relative ( B ) to sexually penetrate, with a part of B ’s body (other than B ’s penis, if B is a man) or anything else, the vagina or anus, as the case may be, of A , knowing that B is a close family relative, shall be guilty of an offence. (2) For the purposes of subsection (1), B is a close family relative of A if B is A ’s grandchild, child, sibling, half‑sibling, parent or grandparent (whether such relationship is or is not traced through lawful wedlock). (3) A person who is guilty of an offence under this section shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 5 years. 376H.section_title: Procurement of sexual activity by deception or false representation

raw.marginal_note: Procurement of sexual activity by deception or false representation
raw.text: —(1) Any person ( A ) shall be guilty of an offence if — ( a ) A intentionally touches another person ( B ) or intentionally incites B to — ( b ) the touching is sexual and B consents to the touching; ( c ) A fraudulently obtains B ’s consent by means of deception or false representation practised or made by A for that purpose; ( d ) the deception or false representation mentioned in paragraph ( c ) relates to — ( e ) A knows or has reason to believe that the consent was given in consequence of such deception or false representation. (2) A person who is guilty of an offence under subsection (1) shall — (3) For the purposes of subsection (1) —

sub_items.text: —(1) Any person ( A ) shall be guilty of an offence if — ( a ) A intentionally touches another person ( B ) or intentionally incites B to — ( b ) the touching is sexual and B consents to the touching; ( c ) A fraudulently obtains B ’s consent by means of deception or false representation practised or made by A for that purpose; ( d ) the deception or false representation mentioned in paragraph ( c ) relates to — ( e ) A knows or has reason to believe that the consent was given in consequence of such deception or false representation.

(2) A person who is guilty of an offence under subsection (1) shall — ( a ) in the case where the sexual touching mentioned in that subsection involved — ( b ) in any other case, be punished on conviction with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 2 years, or with fine, or with both.

(3) For the purposes of subsection (1) — ( a ) a person makes a false representation if it is untrue or misleading, and that person knows that it is, or might be, untrue or misleading; ( b ) a representation may be express or implied; and ( c ) a “sexually protective measure” means —

377.section_title: Sexual penetration, etc., of a corpse

raw.marginal_note: Sexual penetration, etc., of a corpse
raw.text: —(1) A person ( A ) who — ( a ) penetrates, with A ’s penis, the vagina, anus or mouth of a human corpse; ( b ) sexually penetrates, with a part of A ’s body (other than A ’s penis, if A is a man) or anything else, the vagina or anus of a human corpse; ( c ) causes A ’s vagina, anus or mouth to be penetrated by the penis of a human corpse; ( d ) causes A ’s vagina or anus to be sexually penetrated by a part of a human corpse (other than the penis, if the human corpse is that of a deceased man); ( e ) causes a person ( B ) to penetrate, with B ’s penis (if B is a man), the vagina, anus or mouth of a human corpse, without B ’s consent; ( f ) causes B to sexually penetrate, with a part of B ’s body (other than B ’s penis, if B is a man) or anything else, the vagina or anus of a human corpse, without B ’s consent; ( g ) causes B ’s vagina, anus or mouth to be penetrated by the penis of a human corpse, without B ’s consent; or ( h ) causes B ’s vagina or anus to be sexually penetrated by a part of a human corpse (other than the penis, if the human corpse is that of a deceased man), without B ’s consent, shall be guilty of an offence. (2) A person who is guilty of an offence under subsection (1)( a ), ( b ), ( c ) or ( d ) shall be punished with imprisonment for a term that may extend to 5 years, or with fine, or with both. (3) A person who is guilty of an offence under subsection (1)( e ), ( f ), ( g ) or ( h ) shall be punished with imprisonment for a term that may extend to 20 years, and shall also be liable to fine or to caning.

sub_items.text: —(1) A person ( A ) who — ( a ) penetrates, with A ’s penis, the vagina, anus or mouth of a human corpse; ( b ) sexually penetrates, with a part of A ’s body (other than A ’s penis, if A is a man) or anything else, the vagina or anus of a human corpse; ( c ) causes A ’s vagina, anus or mouth to be penetrated by the penis of a human corpse; ( d ) causes A ’s vagina or anus to be sexually penetrated by a part of a human corpse (other than the penis, if the human corpse is that of a deceased man); ( e ) causes a person ( B ) to penetrate, with B ’s penis (if B is a man), the vagina, anus or mouth of a human corpse, without B ’s consent; ( f ) causes B to sexually penetrate, with a part of B ’s body (other than B ’s penis, if B is a man) or anything else, the vagina or anus of a human corpse, without B ’s consent; ( g ) causes B ’s vagina, anus or mouth to be penetrated by the penis of a human corpse, without B ’s consent; or ( h ) causes B ’s vagina or anus to be sexually penetrated by a part of a human corpse (other than the penis, if the human corpse is that of a deceased man), without B ’s consent, shall be guilty of an offence.

sub_items.label: (a)
sub_items.text: penetrates, with A ’s penis, the vagina, anus or mouth of a human corpse;

sub_items.label: (b)
sub_items.text: sexually penetrates, with a part of A ’s body (other than A ’s penis, if A is a man) or anything else, the vagina or anus of a human corpse;

sub_items.label: (c)
sub_items.text: causes A ’s vagina, anus or mouth to be penetrated by the penis of a human corpse;

sub_items.label: (d)
sub_items.text: causes A ’s vagina or anus to be sexually penetrated by a part of a human corpse (other than the penis, if the human corpse is that of a deceased man);

sub_items.label: (e)
sub_items.text: causes a person ( B ) to penetrate, with B ’s penis (if B is a man), the vagina, anus or mouth of a human corpse, without B ’s consent;

sub_items.label: (f)
sub_items.text: causes B to sexually penetrate, with a part of B ’s body (other than B ’s penis, if B is a man) or anything else, the vagina or anus of a human corpse, without B ’s consent;

sub_items.label: (g)
sub_items.text: causes B ’s vagina, anus or mouth to be penetrated by the penis of a human corpse, without B ’s consent; or

sub_items.label: (h)
sub_items.text: causes B ’s vagina or anus to be sexually penetrated by a part of a human corpse (other than the penis, if the human corpse is that of a deceased man), without B ’s consent,
(2) A person who is guilty of an offence under subsection (1)( a ), ( b ), ( c ) or ( d ) shall be punished with imprisonment for a term that may extend to 5 years, or with fine, or with both.

(3) A person who is guilty of an offence under subsection (1)( e ), ( f ), ( g ) or ( h ) shall be punished with imprisonment for a term that may extend to 20 years, and shall also be liable to fine or to caning.

377BA.section_title: Word or gesture intended to insult modesty of any person

raw.marginal_note: Word or gesture intended to insult modesty of any person
raw.text: Whoever, intending to insult the modesty of any person, utters any word, makes any sound or gesture, or exhibits any object, intending that such word or sound will be heard, or that such gesture or object will be seen by such person, or intrudes upon the privacy of such person, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both.

    Alternative requirement (any_of): proving one following limb is enough:
      Conjunctive requirement (all_of): every following limb must be proved:
        Alternative requirement (any_of): proving one following limb is enough:
          Physical element (actus reus): Utters any word
          Physical element (actus reus): Makes any sound
        Mental element (mens rea): Intending that such word or sound will be heard by such person
      Conjunctive requirement (all_of): every following limb must be proved:
        Alternative requirement (any_of): proving one following limb is enough:
          Physical element (actus reus): Makes any gesture
          Physical element (actus reus): Exhibits any object
        Mental element (mens rea): Intending that such gesture or object will be seen by such person
      Physical element (actus reus): Intrudes upon the privacy of such person

Penalty:
  Shall be punished with imprisonment for a term of not less than no time and not more than 1 year.

metadata.summary: Creates an offence for insulting a person's modesty through specified words, sounds, gestures, objects, or privacy intrusion.

raw.text: —(1) Any person ( A ) shall be guilty of an offence who — ( a ) intentionally observes another person ( B ) doing a private act without B ’s consent; and ( b ) knows or has reason to believe that B does not consent to being observed. (2) Any person ( A ) shall be guilty of an offence who — (3) Any person ( A ) shall be guilty of an offence who — (4) Any person ( A ) shall be guilty of an offence who — (5) Any person ( A ) shall be guilty of an offence who — (6) Any person ( A ) who installs equipment, or constructs or adapts a structure or part of a structure, with the intention of enabling A or another person to commit an offence under subsection (1), (2), (3), (4) or (5) shall be guilty of an offence. (7) Subject to subsection (8), a person who is guilty of an offence under this section shall on conviction be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 2 years, or with fine, or with caning, or with any combination of such punishments. (8) A person who commits an offence under this section against a person who is below 14 years of age shall on conviction be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 2 years and shall also be liable to fine or to caning. (9) In any proceedings for an offence under this section, where a person ( A ) has made a recording of another person ( B ) doing a private act or of B ’s genital region, breasts if B is female, or buttocks (whether exposed or covered), in circumstances where the genital region, breasts or buttocks would not otherwise be visible, it is presumed until the contrary is proved that B did not consent to A making the recording.

377BC.section_title: Distribution of voyeuristic image or recording

raw.marginal_note: Distribution of voyeuristic image or recording
raw.text: —(1) Any person ( A ) shall be guilty of an offence who — ( a ) intentionally or knowingly distributes an image or recording of another person ( B ) without B ’s consent to the distribution; ( b ) knowing or having reason to believe that the image or recording was obtained through the commission of an offence under section 377BB; and ( c ) knows or has reason to believe that B does not consent to the distribution. (2) Any person ( A ) shall be guilty of an offence who — (3) Subject to subsection (4), a person who is guilty of an offence under subsection (1) or (2) shall on conviction be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 5 years, or with fine, or with caning, or any combination of such punishments. (4) Where the image or recording in subsection (1) or (2) is of a person below 14 years of age, a person who is guilty of an offence under subsection (1) or (2) shall on conviction be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 5 years, and shall also be liable to fine or to caning.

(2) Any person ( A ) shall be guilty of an offence who — ( a ) intentionally or knowingly has in his possession an image or recording of another person ( B ) for the purpose of distribution without B ’s consent to the distribution; ( b ) knowing or having reason to believe that the image or recording was obtained through the commission of an offence under section 377BB; and ( c ) knows or has reason to believe that B does not consent to the distribution.
